Text.zh: 相信我，通灵师比他们的手下更加令人毛骨悚然。

Text.en: <size:31>{A wolf does not bite his mate as he does his prey, yet both begin with bared teeth. Know who you are, and you will know the meaning of the bite.}
Text.zh: 虽有尖牙，狼会撕咬它的猎物而不是同伴。知道你自己是谁，明白撕咬的意义。

Text.en: Sometimes your greatest enemy is the only one keeping you breathing.
Text.zh: 一个所谓的宿命之敌，最后可能会成为你活下去的唯一理由。

Text.en: You gave us life and love, more than you could spare. Now you're gone, and we are stranded, alone, cold and without purpose.
Text.zh: 你曾无私地给予我们生命和博爱。现在你的离去让我们的存在孤独又毫无意义。

Text.en: Maligaro may have valued function over form, but that did not mute his flair for the dramatic.
Text.zh: 马雷格罗可能注重内在忽视外表，但这不能掩盖他对夸张创造的狂热。

Text.en:
Never dying, yet not living,
Endlessly they wander
beneath the harvest moon.
Text.zh: 死不去也活不了，他们在凄惨的月光下没有目的，没有尽头地漫游着。

Text.en: When sky meets ground the flames can be found, but who has the will to tame them.
Text.zh: 当天空与地面相撞，碰撞的火花美丽动人，但却无人能够征服他们。

Text.en: <size:31>{Publicly, he lived a pious and chaste life of poverty. Privately, tithes and tributes made him and his lascivious company very comfortable indeed.}
Text.zh: 在众人面前，他是负有盛名的诗人，一生追求诗甫。在他的内心之中，他渴望的是最平静的生活·

Text.en: The Ezomyte, desperate for aid, cried out to the gods.
Text.zh: 艾兹麦人绝望无助之际，神灵是他们最后的救赎·

Text.en:
"The most valuable treasures should be hidden in plain sight." 
- Ina, Keeper of Goods
Text.zh:
“最珍贵的宝物，更应当藏在意想不到的平凡之处。”
— 伊娜，货物保管员

Text.en: TBC
Text.zh: 你不会想知道的·

Text.en: The higher you soar, the further you must fall.
Text.zh: 爬得越高，摔得越疼·

Text.en: The pirates of Pondium know that full pockets have a tendency to sink.
Text.zh: 装的越满的海盗船，就越是危险·

Text.en:
Friendship worth its weight in gold,
a treasure every day.
I thought together we'd grow old,
until fate pulled you away.
Text.zh: 友谊就像是每天都在获得无法估量的财富·

Text.en:
You were always there, by my side,
whether we sat or played.
Our friendship was a joyful ride,
I wish you could have stayed.
Text.zh: 步子迈得太大，连思想都有点跟不上了。

Text.en:
The sea strikes the rock relentlessly.
Whether in one day or in ten thousand years,
eventually the rock will crumble,
and the Brine King's domain will grow.
Text.zh:
海水击岩石，
一日复一日，
待到洞穿时，
惊海之王兴。

Text.en:
Malachai ran roughshod over every ethical boundary in pursuit of creating the ideal gemling.
For him, there was no doubt that the end would justify the means.
Text.zh:
玛拉凯为创造理想的宝石人愿意跨过所有的道德界限。
对他来说，结果毫无疑问的要比手段的正当性重要。

Text.en:
After years of subterfuge, sabotage, and sacrifice,
Sarn had become a powder keg just waiting to explode.
Finally, Victario lit the match.
Text.zh:
经过多年的精心准备、暗中破坏和忍辱负重，萨恩终于被反抗军变成了一个即将爆炸的火药桶。
最后，维多利奥点燃了引信。

Text.en:
Even as the tide turned, and victory swiftly became impossible,
the legionnaires of Lioneye's Watch stood their ground and fought on,
every last man dying a brutal, but honourable death.
Text.zh:
即便战局逆转，胜利变得遥不可及，
狮眼守望的士兵依旧坚守阵地、奋力拼杀，
他们每个人都下场凄惨，却又死得其所。

Text.en:
Cadiro watched young Chitus play in the frostbitten grass, care-free, happy,
and unburdened by the responsibility the Perandus scion would soon endure.
Text.zh:
卡迪罗注视着年轻的切图斯在结霜的青草中无忧无虑地嬉戏，
不禁为他即将承担起的普兰德斯贵族义务感到担忧。

Text.en:
As Malachai stepped into the Highgate mines,
cold winds billowed from the mountain above.
One final, feeble attempt to save what was meant to be eternal.
Text.zh:
玛拉凯踏入统治者之殿下方的矿井，
冷冽的山风不停地吹拂着。
这是他最后一次尝试拯救所谓的永恒之物。

Text.en:
News of Kaom's victory in the south spread quickly.
Those in support of the coming rebellion bided their time.
The rest fled like rats from a fire.
Text.zh:
冈姆在南方取得胜利的消息很快传开来。
叛乱的支持者欢呼雀跃。
帝国的支持者作鸟兽散。

Text.en:
The winter that followed the Cataclysm was especially harsh.
Wraeclast's few survivors fought to the death for the last scraps of bread.
Text.zh:
灾变后的冬天极为严酷。
瓦尔克拉斯内为数不多的幸存者为了仅剩的面包争得个你死我活。

Text.en:
Emperor Chitus could offer you a gift with one hand,
and drive a blade into your back with the other.
His blend of brutality and charisma cultured a potent 
mixture of fear and admiration among the masses.
Text.zh:
切图斯皇帝一手拿着精美的礼品，一手握着背刺的暗刃。
他的魅力和残忍不仅让大众心生恐惧，也让他们由衷钦佩。

Text.en:
The Maraketh knew that a mountain path
free of vegetation was cut by regular avalanches,
and so the advancing Eternals were unwittingly drawn into a deathtrap.
Text.zh:
马拉克斯人清楚山路没有植被是因为经常发生雪崩，
于是永恒帝国便不知不觉地向着一个致命陷阱进军。

Text.en:
At night, as a young Daresso shared his bed with a woman whose name he could not recall,
his thoughts remained in the one place he called his own: The Grand Arena.
Text.zh:
某天晚上，一个年轻的德瑞索在和一个他早已忘记姓名的女人同床共枕时，
他的思绪却停留在他唯一的归宿：大竞技场。

Text.en:
The infamous Weylam Roth was never afraid
to push the boundaries of Templar authority,
and was never deterred when the Templar pushed back.
Text.zh:
臭名昭著的威勒姆·罗斯从不怕冒犯圣堂武僧，
更不怕他们的报复。

Text.en:
Izaro gave rise to Chitus.
Chitus gave rise to Voll.
None could know how it would end.
None could stop it.
Text.zh:
伊泽洛造就了切图斯，
切图斯造就了沃尔。
没人知道一切会如何结束。
也没人能阻止。

Text.en:
Exposure led to the deaths of many slaves.
And it was the duty of slaves to bury the dead in the frozen ground.
A growing circle of misery and death 
that the elite were all too happy to sustain.
Text.zh:
严寒夺去了许多奴隶的性命。
而在冰天雪地中掩埋尸体也是奴隶的义务。
如此悲剧的死亡循环在贵族眼中，
不过是一场消遣。

Text.en:
The Cataclysm was but the first act in a play of horrors.
The dead lingered, and the living began to rot.
Text.zh:
灾变不过是这场恐怖表演的第一幕。
死者复苏，生者腐化。

Text.en:
"Rage, malice, hunger - some traits are more easily carried across the barrier of death."
- Kadavrus, Surgeon to the Umbra
Text.zh:
“愤怒、怨恨、饥饿——这是不死者身上最常出现的特质。”
- 黑影薛朗的助手 凯达弗斯

Text.en:
Of the elements at the thaumaturgist's disposal, lightning remained the least understood.
The simple passage of energy from one to another could result in profound power, or rapid death.
Text.zh:
在奇术师所能操控的元素中，
闪电始终是最扑朔迷离的。
这种简单的能量传导行为既能带来强大的力量，也能让活人瞬间化为一具焦尸。

Text.en:
Sarn's crematorium, built amidst the city's slums, 
coated the nearby shanties in a film of ash.
The dwellers grew ill, sputtering and coughing,
and in this way the crematorium guaranteed it would have many customers.
Text.zh: 萨恩的火葬场，建造的如此隐蔽，却见证了历史的变迁·

Text.en:
The Eternal Empire gave few freedoms to the Ezomytes, but fostered their talents as archers. 
At auction, an Ezomyte slave's value was only as good as their aim.
Text.zh:
永恒的帝国无法给于任何人自由。
即便是永恒帝国的君王们亦是如此·

Text.en:
You have been found guilty.
Let the fires cleanse you of your sins.
Text.zh:
罪行确凿，
当处火刑。

Text.en:
"A master artist takes his time when he's painting.
Unfortunately, my kind doesn't often have that luxury."
- Coralito, Brotherhood of Silence
Text.zh:
“艺术大师在绘画时往往会慢慢的精心雕琢。
可惜，我这样的人无法享受这份闲适。”
- 寂静联盟克拉里多

Text.en:
"The Emperor's trusted surgeons are not the only ones who 
practice the dark arts. Some of us are just more discreet."
Text.zh:
“这个国家里研究黑魔法的并不只皇帝的心腹医师，
我们有些人也有涉猎，只不过研究时更谨慎而已。”

Text.en: What mother wouldn't give her life for that of her children?
Text.zh: 哪个母亲不会为了孩子付出自己的生命呢？

Text.en:
"Impossibly heavy, yet the ancient edge is as keen as any modern blade's.
A reminder that, in the end, when we lie in the bellies of worms,
it is what we make that makes us."
- Icius Perandus, Antiquities Collection, Item 47
Text.zh:
"难以置信的沉重。蕴含了历史气息的剑柄与近代剑刃的完美融合。 
又仿佛在时刻提醒着我, 懒惰就会被腐蚀， 
即使是最锋利的剑刃也无法幸免。
你现在的所作所为, 也将决定你未来的位置。" 
- - 伊席厄斯.普兰德斯, 第47号藏品

Text.en:
"To touch it is to be overwhelmed with the need to carve your name into history.
A face no one remembers, yet once you have seen it, you will never forget."
- Icius Perandus, Antiquities Collection, Item 48
Text.zh:
“触摸它，便会被铭刻己名于历史的冲动所淹没。
一张无人记得的面孔，可一旦见过，便永难忘怀。”
— 伊席厄斯·普兰德斯，《珍奇古玩大典》，藏品第48号

Text.en:
A strong pair of legs may carry you far,
but a silver tongue and a few brutes
can take you to the edge of the world.
Text.zh:
强健的双腿可以带你远行，走遍大地，
但是智慧和领导才华能带你征服整个世界·

Text.en:
Don't hesitate; bring death to all, conclusively and swiftly,
or they will give you the same treatment.
Text.zh: 不要犹豫，一瞬间的迟疑可能带给你的就是死亡·

Text.en:
After the fires swept down from the sky and swallowed the city,
all that lingered, as if locked in time,
was a memory of that which was gone, a whisper of deeds undone.
Text.zh: 火焰吞噬了天空和城市。记忆已经逝去，历史依然存在·

Text.en:
A sharp and heavy beat,
discorded, out of tune,
when you hear it on the wind,
you know death will follow soon.
Text.zh:
锋利与沉重的跳动，
不和调的嘈杂，
传自风中的震动，
死亡紧随而来。

Text.en:
Whiff of cold, tiny spark, faintest flicker in the dark.
Embers swirl, ice takes form, sky exposed - Death's perfect storm.
Frost and thunder, flames shine bright, ruin walks the land tonight.
By your hand they dance and bend, wield them and brook no end.
Text.zh:
寒冷气息，渺小火光，黑暗中额外明亮·
余火旋转，冰霜自燃，死亡风暴转瞬就到·
霜与雷，火与光，今夜将陆地燃成荒·
你手中，皆臣服，征服战火愿天不亮·

Text.en:
To discover how cruel nature can be,
you need only shake the wrong branch.
Text.zh: 自然有多残酷?摇晃一束错误的花枝就能告诉你结果·

Text.en: Some things must die so that others can live.
Text.zh: 有的人必须死，为了更多的人可以存活·

Text.en:
"He does not love. He does not feel regret or remorse.
He does not think about consequences, only possibilities.
If that does not describe a monster, tell me: what does?"
- Archbishop Geofri
Text.zh:
"他不明白爱。他不懂得悔·
他不考虑代价，只要他认为可行·
如果这还不能描述一个怪物，告诉我什么才是怪物?"
- 大主教吉尔菲

Text.en:
Ask any tracker and they'll tell you:
the most dangerous animals
are the ones who don't bother to hide.
Text.zh:
询问任何猎人，他们都会告诉你：
最危险的猎物，
是比你更有耐心的猎物·

Text.en:
"Decomposition takes hold too quickly!
No, if we are to keep the specimen in perfect shape,
we must begin the process before expiry."
Text.zh:
"腐烂的发生是如此之快！
不，我们必须让样品处于完美的状态，
必须在它们过期之前开始所有的程序·"

Text.en:
"Nothing is more natural than competition.
One feasts, another starves.
One blossoms, another withers.
And one cannot catch the golden sunlight
without casting a bitter shadow."
- Cadiro Perandus
Text.zh:
"竞争就是自然的法则·
一个人的饱餐，就是另一个人的挨饿·
一个人的盛开，就是另一个人的凋零·
没有体会过最苦暗的阴影就无法捕捉到最闪耀的光辉·"
- 卡迪罗普兰德斯

Text.en:
"There seems to be some innate relationship between the forces of
chaos and cold. Perhaps we should consult an occultist."
Text.zh:
“看来寒冷和混沌之间有一些内在联系。
我们也许可以咨询一位玄学家。”

Text.en:
Though the path we walk is the same,
each journey is different.
Text.zh: 即使我们走过相同的道路，我们的旅程也将充满不同的挑战·

Text.en:
"Prototype #56 was deemed safe, but judging by Administrator Qotra's new hustle
and improved ability to micromanage us, I'm not so sure." 
- Researcher Graven
Text.zh:
“五十六号实验底子似乎很安全。但从总管科塔拉最近的忙碌和我们细节管理能力的提升来看，似乎又不是这么回事。”
——研究员葛拉文

Text.en:
You do not cease to be when you die
any more than the caterpillar ceases to be when it cocoons.
Come, let me show you.
Text.zh:
当有人去世时，并不减少人生的趣味。
就好像有人欢笑时，并不减少认真的态度。

Text.en:
"Prototype #73 is a near perfect opposite of the original. Perhaps our failures
are due to some unknown polarity we have yet to discover."
Text.zh:
“七十三号实验底子几乎跟原版完全相反。
也许还有未知的一极导致了我们的失败。”

Text.en:
The impediments of flesh are weak.
The air we breathe knows no such boundaries.
I exhale, and you inhale,
and inexorably we inch towards oneness.
Text.zh:
残缺的生命是弱小的·
即使我们呼吸的相同的空气也不能改变这种区别·
我在吸气，而你只有呼气·
我们注定只有我会存活·

Text.en:
Feed a beast and it will not hunt.
Protect it and it will not fight.
Ferocity must be learned, not taught.
It is suffering that forges the greatest warriors.
Text.zh:
喂食野兽，它就不会伤害你·
保护它野兽 它就不会与你为敌·
暴行最易被模仿却无法被教授·
只有疼痛能够打造最强的战士·

Text.en:
He took me by my hand, promised me power beyond compare.
But I did not do it for power.
I did it for love.
And I'd do it again in an instant.
Text.zh:
"他握了我的手，许诺我无与伦比的力量·
但我并不是为了力量·
我只是为了爱情·
我毫不犹豫·"
—— 达拉

Text.en: What man does not wish for immortality?
Text.zh: 有任何男人可以抗拒永生的诱惑吗?

Text.en:
Whispered memory,
A shimmer in the darkness.
Forgotten no more.
Text.zh:
微语回忆，
暗中微光，
刻骨铭心。

Text.en:
Though flesh may decay, and bones turn to dust, the spirit fades not.
Floundering eternal in pools of tranquil dark.
Text.zh:
尽管肉体会腐朽，骨头会化为灰烬，但灵魂却从不消褪。
在静谧的黑暗之池中永恒挣扎。

Text.en:
Ambition followed progress,
Vanity followed passion,
Hubris followed intellect...
And silence followed all.
Text.zh:
进取萌生野心，
热忱萌生虚荣，
智慧萌生傲慢……
而最终一切都归于沉寂。

Text.en:
I have already lost more
than you shall ever possess.
Text.zh: 我失去的比你拥有的更多。

Text.en:
That which could not be digested
remains lost within, the buried remnants
of whole cultures, of entire worlds.
Text.zh: 那些无法消解之物依旧迷失其中，成为整个文化、整个世界被埋葬的残留物。

Text.en:
Champions of her collection may take what
they wish, so long as they use it to entertain her...
Text.zh: 她的收藏，勇士们可以拿走他们想要的，只要是用藏品来逗乐她...

Text.en:
Minds are not the only thing
the Cleansing Fire keeps eternal...
Text.zh: 心灵不是唯一使焚界者永恒的东西...

Text.en:
There is a place, a state of being,
where all that you love forgets you.
Text.zh: 有一个地方，一种状态，你所爱的一切都忘记了你。

Text.en:
Step into the inner sanctum of the minds of old
Enter a realm untouched even by starlight
Through transcendence, bend reality to your will.
Text.zh:
探索古老灵魂栖息的隐秘圣地
进入连星光都不曾触及的秘境
执掌超凡之力，让现实臣服于你。

Text.en: A great Silence falls...
Text.zh: 一片寂静笼罩...

Text.en:
An heirloom of the champions of Wraeclast
Lies forgotten in an abandoned reliquary
Born of a starry void from which time cannot escape
Text.zh:
瓦尔克拉斯勇士传家之物
静落在荒弃圣物厅之一角
生于浩渺时光之繁星虚无

Text.en:
They knew the truth, but had no choice
Generations hence, perhaps, one day,
there would be a chance
Text.zh:
他们知道真相，却别无选择。
一代又传一代，或许有一日，
时机能够到来

Text.en:
The craftsman rots.
His tools rust.
But his legacy cuts through the ages.
Text.zh:
匠人终逝去，
工具亦锈坏，
其作永流传。

Text.en:
The words of a man, delicate like life, pass on feebly into forgotten verse,
but his creations... his craft shall be championed agelessly.
Text.zh:
人类的言语就像脆弱的生命，在遗忘的诗篇中烟消云散，
但他的作品……他的作品将永远高不可攀。

Text.en:
Time is a river, forking forever into infinite unknowns.
Nothing is ever truly gone.
It is merely taking a different route to the sea.
Text.zh:
时间漫漫如河流，永无止尽分支流。
万事万物皆尽在，
千途万径汇海流。

Text.en:
It is not regret that stalks me now...
merely the child of my sins.
Text.zh: 现在困扰我的不是后悔...仅仅是我罪孽的孩子。

Text.en: Remember us.
Text.zh: 记住我们吧。

Text.en:
You are not the first champion to receive this gift,
and you will likely not be the last.
Text.zh: 你不是第一个收到这份礼物的勇士，也可能不是最后一个。

Text.en:
To a being of the stars, Time is irrelevant,
and memories are but a collection of present moments.
Text.zh: 对于星球上的生物来说，时间无关紧要，记忆只是当下时刻的集合。

Text.en:
Though you might not see it, a sliver of my soul remains,
and I am trying with all my strength to get back to you.
Text.zh: 虽然你可能看不到，但我还有一丝灵魂尚存，我正竭力回到你身边。

Text.en:
You did not slay me.
You saved me.
I forgive you.
Text.zh: 你没有杀我。你救了我。我原谅你。

Text.en: There are holes in the sky...
Text.zh: 天空出现了孔洞...

Text.en: Time stands still, and a new champion ascends.
Text.zh: 光阴永恒流转，新晋崭露头角。

Text.en:
We cannot fight the gods today, nor tomorrow
Take this, our children of Time, and free mankind
Text.zh:
不论今时，或是明日，神皆难以匹敌。
收下它吧，时光之子，去解救人类吧。

Text.en:
All creatures have the potential for greatness
or unequivocal failure.
Text.zh: 所有生物都有干成大事或是贻笑大方的潜质。

Text.en:
Vaal bloodpriests were among the earliest intellectuals on record. 
It was they who found that a newly freed soul would 
desperately cling to any other source of life.
Text.zh:
瓦尔血祭司是史料可考的最早出现的知识分子。
他们最先发现刚脱离躯壳的灵魂会不顾一切地附身在其他生命形式上。

Text.en:
"Curious. The test spike penetrated the subject's flesh, but the chest wound
was not as deep as expected. There is something here..."
Text.zh:
“奇怪。实验尖刺贯穿了实验对象的血肉，但胸部的伤口却没有预料的深。
肯定有其它原因……”

Text.en:
Silent, silent night,
Quench the holy light.
Text.zh:
寂静无声的平安夜，
圣神洁白的火炎灭。

Text.en:
Eternal soldiers fight for glory or death.
The Solaris Vanguard know only glory.
Text.zh:
永恒帝国的战士为了荣耀可以战死。
日耀女神的守卫为了荣耀可以不顾一切。

Text.en: The mind had much to endure before the advent of thaumaturgy.
Text.zh: 即使在奇术出现前，人心也有许多顾虑。

Text.en: Wealth can, in fact, buy happiness.
Text.zh: 事实上，财富真的能买到幸福。

Text.en:
'Lightning never strikes the same place twice'
is really just wishful thinking.
Text.zh:
“闪电绝不会命中相同的地方两次”
这不过是凡人的一厢情愿罢了。

Text.en: In the prison of all sins, will you grasp godhood or sorrow? Only hope lies under the shadows.
Text.zh:
在罪恶的监牢中, 
是升天成神还是悲痛坠地. 
暗影之下, 唯有希望.

Text.en:
In combat, a warrior must first be resourceful,
and second, decisive.
Text.zh:
在战斗中，真正的勇士首先要足智多谋，
而后就是坚决果断。

Text.en: <size:31>{There is no mistake so great that it cannot be undone.}
Text.zh: 这个世界上没有错误会巨大到无法弥补。

Text.en: If you wish to rush into strange places, be prepared to face strange things.
Text.zh:
如果你急着想冲进陌生的地方，
就要准备好面对稀奇古怪的事情。

Text.en:
Some powers are far too great even for the
gods of old.
Text.zh: 这是对于远古神灵来说也过于强大的力量。

Text.en: Lethal, untouchable, keen, aflame. Just what I was looking for.
Text.zh:
致命, 不可触摸,
热情, 兴奋跃动。
这就是我所寻找的。

Text.en:
<size:30>{Many strive for greatness,
but it is challenge, unforeseen,
that forges heroes.}

He shattered Her smile
Scattered the fragments like ash
All she did was laugh
Text.zh:
他告诉了我什么是微笑，
而又夺走了我的一切。

Text.en: A lust for souls. The urge to kill just to satisfy its thirst. An unbearable burden that would make even the purest heart blacken over time.
Text.zh:
灵魂的欲望，
是一种为了满足渴望而不顾一切的冲动，
是一种难以承受的负担，
是即使是最纯洁的心也会随着时间的流逝而改变的真相。

Text.en: Our world, dangerous as it is, is far safer than what lies just beyond our sight.
Text.zh:
我们的国度虽然危险，
但远比我们看不到的地方安全得多。

Text.en:
<size:19>{Fourfold the refrain, fortissimo cheer.
One hundred rent twain, naught left for a bier.
The song rattles on, though hollow a bit.
See the hope is gone, no prize shall ye get.}
Text.zh:
<size:19>{四重高歌，欢呼雀跃
一百银钱，棺柩不存。
高歌往复，空空如也。
希望破灭，一无所获。}

Text.en: Some things should not be used. Some power is too great a risk.
Text.zh:
一些东西永远不该被使用，
一些力量同样带着太大的风险。

Text.en: Ever get the feeling you're being watched...?
Text.zh: 你有感到过被人暗中监视的感受吗？

Text.en:
<size:29>{A dark note drips 
from the dreamer's lips, 
A honeyed melody. 
We stand, we fall 
on his beck and call, 
for in his dream we're free.}
Text.zh:
一个黑暗的音符从梦想家的唇角滴下，
成为一个亲昵的旋律。
我们站着，听命于他，
因为在他的梦里，我们是自由的。

Text.en: Its body knows no limits. Its appetite knows no bounds.
Text.zh:
它的身体是无限的。
它的胃口是无穷的。

Text.en: Beware the combatant who shows no confidence, yet still enters the ring, for they are surely hiding something.
Text.zh:
小心那些失去信念的战士，
他们仍然愿意跨进异界，
一定是因为他们藏着什么不为人知的目的。

Text.en: When the darkness weighs upon you, grow light from within.
Text.zh:
当黑暗压在你身上时，
光明将从你的内心成长。

Text.en: As countless fractures weaken the divide, nightmares seep through from the other side.
Text.zh:
无数的打击只会让人更加强硬，
不断的噩梦只会悄悄远离。

Text.en:
<size:27>{A wanderer in the wild strives 
Against countless foes he survives 
A fool pursues a quest contrived 
By the gift of the witch, alive.}
Text.zh:
<size:27>{游荡荒野，孤身无助
逃出生天，斩敌无数
明知崎岖，愚心不改
魔法傍身，神灵庇护}

Text.en: The threads of fate are the strongest ties.
Text.zh: 命运缠成解不开的结。

Text.en: The jeweller's true talent is the capacity to see a jewel's beauty before they draw it out.
Text.zh: 辨玉于石中，乃珠宝匠之真境界。

Text.en: A dream from which you cannot awake is nothing other than a nightmare.
Text.zh: 让你无法挣脱的梦境，是为梦魇。

Text.en: In a world filled with chaos, he could always seek comfort in the hands of his master.
Text.zh: 在混乱的世界，他总能在主宰的手中得到慰藉。

Text.en: Fall into endless night; a voyage into an ocean of oblivion.
Text.zh: 堕入无尽的夜；驶向湮灭的海。

Text.en:
<size:29>{"Fate was always my nemesis... 
But it didn't stop me then, and it won't stop me now."
- Kjetilbrann, The Undaunted}
Text.zh:
<size:29>{“命运曾是我的天罚……
但它过去阻挡不了我，现在也不能。”
— 无畏者杰特尔布兰}

Text.en: Lucian lost himself in ancient scrolls and found in those scrolls a love whose power bridged a millennium.
Text.zh: 路西安醉心于翻阅上古的卷轴，发现曾有一份爱连接了千年。

Text.en:
<size:26>{Many were slain by the sword king 
Their blood decorating his ring 
Those who retreated were slaughtered 
Those who fought bravely were honoured}
Text.zh:
<size:26>{剑圣的剑下亡魂众多
剑圣的戒指鲜血染红
后退者只配被屠戮
奋战者方能得到敬意}

Text.en:
<size:28>{I gave my mind without a fight, 
The twelfth hour I lost control. 
The day is gone but there is light, 
eyes that glow like red-hot coal.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{我将身心尽数交付，
第十二个小时自身难控。
白昼转夜仍有光在，
那是血红的眼让人心怵。}

Text.en: Stare into its depths too long, and you may lose yourself entirely.
Text.zh: 不要久久凝视它的深邃，否则你可能会彻底失去自我。

Text.en: There are countless worlds, but only those with limitless potential will be able to achieve perfection.
Text.zh: 世界万千，但只有无限潜力者才能至臻完美。

Text.en: All dreams vanish when the dreamers wake.
Text.zh: 苏醒之时，梦去之时。

Text.en:
<size:29>{Shrieking souls locked away 
Hate splits frozen cells asunder 
The knot of corruption
undone at last}
Text.zh:
<size:29>{尖啸之魂被沉枷重锁
怨恨则将冰封的牢笼打破
腐化的绳结
最终得以解开}

Text.en: At night, when the seas are calm, and the skies dark, he can see it. The formless fiend that took his eye, and fractured his mind.
Text.zh: 他能看穿夜里寂静的海，幽暗的天。而无形的恶魔夺走了他的眼，粉碎了他的心。

Text.en: Though the years weakened his mind, his body remained deadly as ever.
Text.zh: 岁月衰老了他的心，但肉身依旧致命如初。

Text.en:
A ruler is only as powerful as he is wealthy. 
Control the coin, control the empire.
Text.zh:
拥有财富，可以拥有权力。
掌控经济，可以掌控国力。

Text.en: To create something truly spectacular, you must risk total failure.
Text.zh: 想要真的与众不同，就必须冒天塌之险。

Text.en:
<size:29>{A mind as quick as lightning, 
fists as hard as stone, 
a heart that burns with fury, 
and eyes that chill to the bone.}
Text.zh:
<size:29>{念如闪电，
拳似磐石，
心中有怒，
眼露寒芒。}

Text.en: The village elders had a curious rule: If you hear three voices scream for aid, flee as fast as you can.
Text.zh: 村里的老人们有个规矩：如果听到三个声音在哭喊求救，那么别回头，赶紧逃命。

Text.en: The academic route to knowledge is wide and well-trodden; but it is the untaught one who scales the mountain.
Text.zh: 学院之路引你前往知识之洲；  但只有无师自通者方能移山填海。

Text.en: To know the monster, you must become the monster.
Text.zh: 己欲除魔，必先成魔。

Text.en: His brother would not atone, so he took his brother's life, and with it, his sins.
Text.zh: 他的兄弟无意赎罪，于是他带走了兄弟的生命，这成为了他的原罪。

Text.en: Though the path to divinity is fraught with peril, hope may bloom from within.
Text.zh: 升神之路艰险遍地，希望却在心中萌发。

Text.en: Gaze towards the stars, but beware what gazes back.
Text.zh: 凝视星辰，警惕星辰凝视着你。

Text.en:
Her beauty did not fade 
her humanity did not survive.
Text.zh:
她的美丽并未消退，
但她的人性却并未幸存。

Text.en:
To protect her mansion, she would cut a deal with anyone. Anything. 

Something answered.
Text.zh:
为了保护自己的庄园，她会同任何人、任何事物做交易。 

然后有东西回应了。

Text.en:
<size:28>{Wreathed in the skin and bones
of the beasts she slew, 
her gift came from what she lost: 
her humanity.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{伴随着她屠杀的怪兽
皮囊和骨髓的枯萎， 
她从自己丧失之物那里获得了馈赠， 
她丧失了人性。}

Text.en: The day is dying, the night is born, the air grows cool, the sky is torn.
Text.zh: 白日将尽，黑夜初升，寒风骤起，天空破碎。

Text.en: Nothing can be heard above the din or seen amongst the turmoil. The senses are shattered. All is left to fate.
Text.zh: 无人能在喧嚣中聆听，无人能在混乱中看清方向。感官已经无用。一切只能依托命运。

Text.en: For some, the price of power is never too great.
Text.zh: 总有人愿为力量支付任何代价。

Text.en: The Poet's blood is the Empire's ink.
Text.zh: 帝国的诗篇用诗人的鲜血书写而成。

Text.en: We tried to tell him to get his head checked.
Text.zh: 我们曾试图劝说他做个头部检查。

Text.en: Perfection is the standard, excellence will be handled on a case by case basis.
Text.zh: 完美只是起点，每件作品都该有独特的光芒。

Text.en:
"The process of eternal youth is a give and take. You give them death and take their youth."
- Zerphi of the Vaal
Text.zh:
“长生不老也是一种交换。你送给人们死亡，交换他们的青春。”
——瓦尔的泽佛依

Text.en: All that is flawed is worthless. The slightest error, and this might as well be trash.
Text.zh: 任何有瑕疵的东西都一文不值；完美的世界不允许任何微小的错误，。

Text.en: Fear the man who lusts for power, for he will do anything to get it.
Text.zh: 请对渴望权力的人心生敬畏，因为他会不惜一切。

Text.en: Some gifts are obligations while others are simply opportunities.
Text.zh: 有些天赋是责任，而有些只是给你的机会。

Text.en: Oh the places you will go, the sights you will see, the things you will meet.
Text.zh: 你要去向的地方，你要看到的风景，你要遭遇的人和事。

Text.en: Discovery can lead to beauty, or it can lead to ruin.
Text.zh: 探索之旅可以带来美景，也可能是废墟。

Text.en: Warriors of a distant land, you embark on a journey from which you may not return, but which we will be all the better for.
Text.zh: 远方之地的战士，你将踏上可能一去不返的旅程，但却会为我们带来福祉。

Text.en:
<size:28>{Charmed by beautiful stones 
Consumed with the 
pursuit of perfection 
Ever deeper into the waiting dark}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{被宝石蒙蔽双眼
一心只求圆满
不惜深入黑暗}

Text.en: If enough people believe something to be true, whether or not it is becomes irrelevant.
Text.zh: 只要相信的人多了，是否合理都无关紧要了。

Text.en: Before gemlings, before thaumaturgy, a simple court magician could enthrall the masses.
Text.zh: 在古灵使徒之前，在奇术之前，一个普通的宫廷魔术师就能迷惑大多数人。

Text.en:
<size:28>{"You cannot portray the world using just one colour. Together, you and I, we will paint our world into perfection." 
- Kyra, renegade thaumaturgist}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{“一种颜色怎能描绘世界。你和我一起，我们会共同画出完美的天地。”
- 奇拉，变节的奇术师}

Text.en:
"Only a fool looks to the occult for power and expects to be spared from its wrath." 
- Voll, Emperor of Purity
Text.zh:
“只有蠢货才会向教派寻求权力，并奢望能从它的烈焰中活命。" 
- 帝王福尔

Text.en:
"Sometimes, the most beautiful treasures...are the ones you cannot have." 
- Beryl, Survivor from the Azurite Mines
Text.zh:
“有时候，你得不到的，才是世间最美丽的瑰宝……” 
- 白若，蓝晶矿洞的幸存者

Text.en:
<size:34>{He dispels dark realms
until mortal sufferings
yield to hopeful light.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{他一生驱离黑暗
直到凡人不堪重负
恳求希望之光的饶恕。}

Text.en: Though they were a pack of elite combatants, the Emperor's royal guards were not ready to face one of his notorious parties.
Text.zh: 尽管曾是一群精英斗士，但帝王的皇家护卫仍不敢参加他举办的任何一次胡天胡地的宴会。

Text.en: A life lost, A legacy born.
Text.zh: 人生迷失，遗产浮现。

Text.en:
<size:28>{There's a place where up is down, 
where right is wrong, 
where pleasure is agony, 
and where the living wish only for death.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{有一个地方上下颠倒，
善恶不分，
苦乐交融，
那里的人们只求一死。}

Text.en:
We play at God with our necromancy, 
but forces far more potent 
sleep within these stones.
Text.zh:
我们用亡灵魔法扮演神灵，
但威力过于有效
在石堆下安息吧。

Text.en: "I gave up my body for you. I gave up my name for you. And one day I shall give up my life for you."
Text.zh: 我为你放弃了身体。我为你放弃了名讳。而总有一天，我必将为你放弃生命。

Text.en:
<size:28>{A ravenous mind can readily take in ideas from any source. 
Fortunately for the scholars, he has already learned about sustainability.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{贪婪的头脑饥不择食。
其他学者真幸运，因为他终于学到了什么叫永恒。}

Text.en:
<size:28>{A treasure worth killing for 
is a treasure worth dying for.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{人为财死
鸟为食亡。}

Text.en: Even the most learned man is a fool to his own fate.
Text.zh: 哪怕最博学的人面对自己的命运也只是个愚人。

Text.en:
Upon silent paws and masked by the reeds, 
Farrul's hunt begins as the light recedes.
Text.zh:
利爪无声，芦苇遁形，日光渐隐，
法瑞尔猎行。

Text.en: <size:28>{"The Seas Call, the Mad Answer"}
Text.zh: <size:28>{“大海在召唤，疯狂在回应”}

Text.en: "When grasped in his hand, even an ordinary piece of wood can make the heavens tremble with fear."
Text.zh: ”哪怕普通的木棍握在他的手上，也能让天堂在恐惧中颤抖。“

Text.en: No one ever truly understood what she meant when she said she wanted to raise a family.
Text.zh: 当她说要养家糊口的时候，没人搞清楚那到底意味着什么。

Text.en: <size:28>{You can't seek riches without getting your hands dirty.}
Text.zh: <size:28>{两手干净不可能发家致富。}

Text.en: Sometimes you need to make your own luck.
Text.zh: 偶尔你得靠自己找到运气。

Text.en: Hear me, fish! I love and respect you, but before this day ends I will kill you dead.
Text.zh: 鱼儿啊，请听我一言！我爱你敬你，可日落之前，我必戮你享你。

Text.en:
<size:28>{Every skull was once a person. 
Though 'Who?' is rarely asked. 
Friends, who wear 
the crown of Nook, 
Never need to ask. 
They remember.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{每个头颅都曾有自己的主人，
虽然少有人问津。
朋友们，戴着诺克之冠的头颅
却无需询问。
他们早已铭记在心。}

Text.en: You'll never know the things you miss if you keep your eyes closed
Text.zh: 紧闭双眼，你将永远无法理解错失的一切

Text.en: "Forge me a carapace from their skin, imbued with their soul. Then feed their flesh to the hounds."
Text.zh: “用他们的皮囊为我铸甲，用他们的灵魂淬化，再把他们的血肉丢给猎狗。”

Text.en: Try a thousand times, and eventually you'll have to give up.
Text.zh: 千百遍的尝试，终将换来放弃。

Text.en:
<size:28>{"For all your struggles, all your achievements, even for the greatest of us, a story is all that remains. Write it well." 

-Julius Perandus, Father of Chitus}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{“因为你的一切奋斗，一切成就都将远超我辈，千古名留。因此务必谨慎。” 

—— 尤利乌斯·普兰德斯，切特斯之父}

Text.en: Greed is a hunger that only grows as you feed it
Text.zh: 欲壑难填皆因填

Text.en:
<size:24>{Dread and danger 
makes the air feel thin. 
Above, power slumbers, tempting fate. 
Greed and ambition 
draws countless in, 
For those who seek power 
can never wait.}
Text.zh:
<size:24>{危机遍野，寒意席卷大地。
权力蛰伏，不停撩拨命运。
贪婪无度，片刻不得停息。}

Text.en: In the pursuit of power, as in that of wealth, anything goes.
Text.zh: 追逐权力，正如追求财富，到头两手空空。

Text.en:
Method transcends shape and size. Do something in parallel long enough, and you will always find 
another way.
Text.zh: 方法胜于苦功，历久弥坚，灵光必然乍现。

Text.en: They stand among us, and within us.
Text.zh: 它们与我等同行，也是我等的归宿。

Text.en: The Lord of Chaos dreams as his Eye gazes, unblinking, at his prize. And soon, all shall tremble before his waking form.
Text.zh: 混乱之主睁眼做梦，目不转睛地望着他的奖赏。很快，一切都将在他醒来之前颤抖。

Text.en: A man who hears voices may not necessarily be crazy.
Text.zh: 谜音入脑，未必发狂。

Text.en:
<size:28>{The hatungo know many answers to the same question, 
for time itself is a tangled web.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{那位哈图纳格斯所学包罗万象，
因为时间本身就繁如巨网。}

Text.en:
<size:28>{Souls along a conduit of blood, 
from one vessel to the next.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{鲜血裹挟着灵魂，
逃向新的栖身之地。}

Text.en: Though the forces of nature are mighty and intimidating, it is their absence which should be feared.
Text.zh: 诚然，自然之力深不可测，可当它们消失后才叫人毛骨悚然。

Text.en: In the pursuit of wealth, as in that of power, anything goes.
Text.zh: 追逐权力，正如追求财富，到头两手空空。

Text.en: The howls and cackling could not mask the sadness.
Text.zh: 狂啸与轻笑均无法掩饰悲伤。

Text.en: The line between having not enough to do and too much to do is unusually fine.
Text.zh: 渔线的要领就是不长不短刚刚好。

Text.en: Blessed is the blood of the beholden disciple. The earth is anointed by his step. The damnable are sanctified by his strike.
Text.zh: 蒙恩圣徒以血为福，踏遍千山步履赐祝，弘法除恶众苦皆除。

Text.en: "In the forest again... But at least I have these."
Text.zh: “又见森林……至少我不再两手空空。”

Text.en: In fleeing reality, you step into the realm of madness.
Text.zh: 感受真实，你将步入疯狂之境。

Text.en: "The Karui was correct; there is virtue in honouring my ancestors."
Text.zh: “卡拉说得对，这必将让我光宗耀祖。”

Text.en: They whose lives were given to clothe the Dark Dreamer... They were the lucky ones.
Text.zh: 他们的生命被献给了黑暗梦者……他们真走运。

Text.en:
"There are two ways to pay, Gold and Blood. I'll take my share in both."

-Cameria the Coldblooded
Text.zh:
“酬劳有两种，金子和鲜血。而我两者都要。”

—— 冷血的卡梅莉亚

Text.en: Many sought the Goddess' blessing. Few survived the Labyrinth to receive it.
Text.zh: 寻求女神赐福者众，通过迷宫幸存者寡。

Text.en: However vicious Rhoas might be, a dedicated angler may in time tame them.
Text.zh: 再狠的恐喙鸟，都有适合的棍子叫它们听话。

Text.en: The flesh is a prison, and we are finally free.
Text.zh: 肉体是监牢，我等终自由。

Text.en:
"Set your life on fire. Seek those who fan your flames"

-Rumi of the Vaal
Text.zh:
“点燃你的生命，寻求为你添柴之人。”

—— 瓦尔人鲁米

Text.en: What was once a passing thought tickling the back of the mind is now a desperate, driving desire.
Text.zh: 为何一时突发奇想会变成瘙痒难耐的渴望。

Text.en: When you strive to write your name on the dense pages of History, someone else, inevitably, must be crossed out.
Text.zh: 为了在历史上留下浓重的一笔，必须有人作出牺牲。

Text.en: "Our rulers are being ridiculous, and we are suffering."
Text.zh: “君王着魔，百姓遭殃。”

Text.en: Know thine enemy better than you know thyself.
Text.zh: 知己知彼，百战不殆。

Text.en: Sometimes the best way to achieve greatness is to use a shortcut.
Text.zh: 有时候为了成功免不了走点捷径。

Text.en: Trying to bring your vision to life is enough to drive you mad.
Text.zh: 将幻象融入现实，足以让人发狂。

Text.en:
"I'm going for a walk. I'll be back soon."

 - Blonca's last words
Text.zh:
“我出去散散步，很快就回来。”

——布兰卡的遗言

Text.en:
Born beneath silver light, bearing Lunaris' mark.
Conjuring all his might, the Prodigy turned light to dark.
Text.zh:
生沐银芒，佩月之光，
倾其全力，颠覆阴阳。

Text.en: Time dismantles all things eventually, but some wish to accelerate the process.
Text.zh: 时间终将摧毁一切，但有人渴望将其加速。

Text.en:
<size:30>{A King Loved By All,
A Shadow Awaiting Nightfall,
A Speaker Howling Away,
A Hunter Seeking Prey}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{万众瞩目，
静候日暮，
呼啸如影，
守株待兔}

Text.en: Love is the only redemption after succumbing to the grasps of the Wasteland.
Text.zh: 屈服于这片废土之后，爱是唯一的一丝救赎。

Text.en: Within years of knowledge, distilled by careful craftsmanship, lies great power.
Text.zh: 经年累月，精益求精，力量方成。

Text.en: The Golden Sekhema's words could solve many a feud. Just what form that solution took was another matter.
Text.zh: 金色丝克玛的话语能化解累世仇怨。然而之后却又添新愁。

Text.en:
<size:30>{The artist is long dead,
the lyrics are long forgotten,
yet the Song of War longs to be sung again}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{作者早已死亡，
旋律早被遗忘，
然而这首战歌势必再次唱响。}

Text.en: Chasing purity in the rising light means facing the darkening edges of the spirit.
Text.zh: 朝光明追求纯净如同面对灵魂的黑暗。

Text.en: Where Decay festers, I shall expunge it, and leave in its place a shining monument.
Text.zh: 腐坏所至，我必随行，铸其丰碑，耀我功绩。

Text.en: It is a mortal man's folly to seek power beyond his comprehension.
Text.zh: 追求不可理解的力量正是凡人的悲哀。

Text.en: She took all she had and turned it into something she was craving.
Text.zh: 她取走自己的一切，只为追求自己的渴望。

Text.en: Savour these moments, for they may never return.
Text.zh: 品味这一刻吧，它将不复再来。

Text.en: Some seek to change the world. Others change the world as a consequence of what they seek.
Text.zh: 有些人一辈子都想改变世界，然而有些人在探寻途中就改变了世界。

Text.en:
The mighty huntress always called for her share,
But what we shared was a lifelong bond.
Text.zh:
强大的女猎人总想要她的钱，
但这个钱得拿一辈子来换。

Text.en:
"This was the proudest moment of my life."

"... so far"
Text.zh:
“这是我人生中引以为傲的时刻。”

“……过了这么久。”

Text.en:
<size:28>{I thought love stronger than all,
but time and isolation broke
that which madness could not.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{我以为真爱强过一切，
可时间和孤独却远胜疯狂。}

Text.en: The pain you feel is of no consequence, it is evolution, to be made whole within something greater.
Text.zh: 这并非是为了让你痛苦，而是为了进化，为了让你成为伟大的一份子。

Text.en: The Mists of Madness prey on those who harbour the deepest of fears.
Text.zh: 疯狂的迷雾追寻着那些在深埋恐惧中的猎物。

Text.en:
<size:26>{The pale flame of his heart disappeared in his azure reflection.
The work of a life.
Ambitious, and unfinished.}
Text.zh:
<size:26>{他心中惨白的火焰在蔚蓝色的映衬中消失了。
那是他的毕生心血，雄心勃勃，
却无法完成。}

Text.en:
<size:23>{Vintage or crafted, Glittering and shiny
Dusty or glittering, huge or tiny
Secured within a chest 
or unearthed from your mining
Find jewellery everywhere, 
no need for divining!
So why is it so hard 
to figure out which one to wear?
This one makes me stronger, 
but does it match my hair?}
Text.zh:
<size:23>{精美古董闪闪发亮
拭去尘腐显露金光
大小宝贝尽藏一箱
勘探千里皆我辛劳
故我所获无需献牢！
左思右考为何苦恼
哪件宝贝迎我心坳？
这件珠宝让我显高
可它能否配我发梢？}

Text.en:
<size:26>{"Such dedication for so many years,
all for a couple extra letters at the front of your name..."}
Text.zh:
<size:26>{“毕生奉献，
换来的只有签名前的几行附注。”}

Text.en:
<size:25>{Awoken virtuous slivers of the void,
defiled by the avaricious, 
seized by the tyrannical.}
Text.zh:
<size:25>{空洞的美德将被肢解，
它们被贪婪玷污，
被残暴攫取。}

Text.en:
Even though he found the stash just in time,
He would trade it all away and then some to have him back.
Text.zh: 即使他及时找到这批秘藏，他也宁愿用它们全部来换他生还。

Text.en: Mold the world with your bare hands. Be careful, for what is your doing might become your undoing.
Text.zh: 赤手空拳去创造世界吧。当心，你的行为可能会导致败亡。

Text.en:
<size:26>{They came from far and wide under a common banner.
They sought companionship and competition,
but found a treasure none had foreseen.}
Text.zh:
<size:26>{他们远道而来，扛着共同的大旗。
他们寻求友谊和竞赛，
却找到了意外的宝藏。}

Text.en: If you look hard enough for hidden meaning, you will find it.
Text.zh: 如果你足够努力地寻找隐藏的含义，就会发现它。

Text.en: A good spy doesn't forget she's gone undercover.
Text.zh: 优秀的间谍都懂得悄无声息。

Text.en:
In the dead of night you may elude your shadows,
but they will always find you by dawn.
Text.zh:
夜幕让你逃避阴影，
清晨它将再次上门。

Text.en:
The world is ever-changing.
Once-lush woods now lost beneath shifting sands,
Great cities now drown beneath turbulent waters.
Adapt or perish.
Text.zh:
世界永远在变。
茂林陷入黄沙，
城池沉入波涛。
不变即亡。

Text.en:
<size:25>{They thought winter had come early,
that snow blanketed the land,
but in truth, all their chickens
had come home to roost.}
Text.zh:
<size:25>{他们觉得冬天来得太早，
冰雪笼罩大地，
但事实上，他们的鸡群
早已回家休憩。}

Text.en:
<size:28>{We live to dream of worlds we aren't in,
a false narrative that we use to fill our souls
with doubt;
The world is already beautiful,
there's no need to dream.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{我们生活在不属于我们的梦里，
用虚假的故事填补灵魂的空虚，
我们心有疑惑，
这世界如此美丽，
何需再造梦境。}

Text.en: Even the brightest eventually fade to darkness.
Text.zh: 无上光明也将沦入黑暗。

Text.en:
<size:26>{Some people act as beacons, illuminating the path for others. I was lucky to meet one such person.

- Anton to Zhenya}
Text.zh:
<size:26>{有人如灯塔般照亮世界，我有幸遇见了其中一人。

——安东至曾雅的信}

Text.en:
<size:24>{They would say that he was a dangerous man,
unbound by the sense of morality,
but what does this matter,
when his love for humanity is undeniable
and completion of his work would benefit everyone?}
Text.zh:
<size:24>{有人说他很危险，
毫无道德禁忌，
但这有什么，
难道他不爱全人类，
他的成果不会惠及所有人吗？}

Text.en:
With great power comes...

...Even greater power.
Text.zh:
伟大的力量之后...

...还有更伟大的力量。

Text.en: Why settle when more is never enough.
Text.zh: 既然永不满足，何必停留。

Text.en:
"I know one lady for whose sake I will rip out my heart"

- Derrok, the Dreamer
Text.zh:
“我认识一位女士，情愿为她掏心”

——寻梦者德拉克

Text.en:
The suit I wear,
Is heavy and bare.
A canvas, it seems, breathing life into dreams.
Text.zh:
我的衣服又沉又重。
它就似一面帆，以生命为风，送我如梦。

Text.en: I have a headache, can anyone find me a nurse?
Text.zh: 我头疼欲裂，谁能为我请个护士？

Text.en: <size:26>{The rain pelts down, Mud grasping at his feet, as thoughts of those in his care bolster his resolve.}
Text.zh: <size:26>{暴雨滂沱，足陷深泥，思其牵挂，信念弥坚。}

Text.en:
<size:24>{For the blessed bear in the cave,
A hundred days pass in silence and darkness,
until moon begins its fourth passage,
the bear sheds her beastly fur,
and is reborn, human and whole.}
Text.zh:
<size:24>{赐福之熊，睡于洞中，
百日寂静，黑暗为伴，
待月盈凸，去其毛皮
熊获重生，再造为人。}

Text.en:
<size:25>{Seeking shelter from the night,
in the warmth of the run-down inn,
erstwhile foes put aside the fight,
to bond over whiskey and gin.}
Text.zh:
<size:25>{为避冷夜寻庐庵，
草肆虽残酒却暖，
往日冤仇皆作罢，
交杯换盏俱尽欢。}

Text.en:
<size:29>{Come home to me, my sweet.
Oh, how long you've been!
Lie with me in the frozen dark.
I may yet find forgiveness
Now that fear has left you.}
Text.zh:
<size:29>{快回家吧，宝贝。
原来你已经回来！
快跟我在黑暗中躺下。
我就会原谅你。
恐惧将离你远去。}

Text.en:
<size:28>{No blade conquers sin;
guide a sinner to virtue,
unveil their lost sight—
your eyes, too, see in
everyone the inner child,
no matter their might.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{没有剑刃可以战胜罪恶；
引导一个罪人走向美德，
揭开他遗失的视野——
你的眼睛也将看到
所有人心中的孩童，
无论他们多么强大。}

Text.en:
<size:25>{"Glimmers of benevolence,
Shrouded a lacquered lust for power,
Yet still they followed his influence."}
Text.zh:
<size:25>{“慈悲的光芒，
掩盖了追逐权力的欲望，
却潜移默化被它影响。”}

Text.en: Experience teaches us that the cold is not the same as the absence of warmth.
Text.zh: 寒冷不等于没有温暖，这是经验之谈。

Text.en: "Man may shape the world, but these? These shape the man." -Inquisitor Maligaro
Text.zh: “世界由人塑造，而这里则塑造人。”—— 审判者马利伽罗

Text.en:
<size:27>{"Be fast. That is all that matters."
- Rita of the Asylum}
Text.zh:
<size:27>{“天下之道，唯快不破。”
——收容所的瑞塔}

Text.en: Dost thou lead my men to victory, my child?
Text.zh: 孩子，是你领导我的人取胜的吗？

Text.en: They believed, falsely, that the strongest of generals could outlast eons of corruption.
Text.zh: 他们迅速坚信，只要有最强大的将领，便可不惧永世腐败。

Text.en: One who is blinded by their past may only see a sliver of the present.
Text.zh: 被过去蒙蔽双眼，就只能管中窥探当今。

Text.en: If time is the most valuable currency, how do you make more of it?
Text.zh: 如果时间最值钱，那你怎么挣时间？

Text.en: "The house always wins... except when I do."
Text.zh: “这一家总赢……直到我下场。”

Text.en:
<size:25>{Cede all power and wealth to me and serve me in perpetuity.
A minor inconvenience I assure you.}
Text.zh:
<size:25>{放弃所有权力和财富，永世向我效忠。
我保证这个代价微不足道。}

Text.en:
<size:24>{Plenty enter, dreaming of treasure
Twenty bled, slain as they fled
Shadows loom, half left doomed
The blind cries, five lost eyes
Careless twins, a lich's new prize
One as a decoy, none pass the king
Gehennix feeds only on whispers}
Text.zh:
<size:24>{许多人进来寻觅梦中宝藏
有二十人负伤，在奔逃中命丧
暗影滋生，剩余的必亡
有五人没了眼睛，在黑暗中惨叫
一对粗心的兄弟，进了巫妖的罗网
国王略施小计，人人皆入圈套
葛海尼克斯在低语中举觞}

Text.en: Every obsession began with a single, innocent taste.
Text.zh: 每一次沉迷都从懵懂的尝试开始。

Text.en: "Bino's brief career as a chef at Axiom saved the executioner a lot of work."
Text.zh: “比诺短暂的厨师生涯给刽子手们节约了大量工作。”

Text.en: He appeared before the Light Eater, and at once knew his soul was damned.
Text.zh: 他一来到光明吞噬者面前，就立刻发现自己大难临头。

Text.en: The harvest ended, the floods began.
Text.zh: 收割结束，洪水开始。

Text.en: The swarm began with just a couple of children. Now, it is her entire existence.
Text.zh: 蜂群一开始只有几个孩子，如今已经是她的一切生命。

Text.en: <size:26>{The strange voice showed Aul a future where his legacy was forgotten, where new cultures broke themselves upon Aul's ruined world.}
Text.zh: <size:26>{陌生的声音向奥尔展示了一个未来，在那里他所有遗产都统统遭到遗忘，在奥尔的世界化为废墟之后，诞生了一种全新的文化。}

Text.en:
<size:28>{Through the eyes of a person, 
you can see their soul
Through the eyes of a jewel, 
you can see the universe}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{通过人的眼睛可以直视灵魂
通过珠宝的眼睛可以看到整个宇宙}

Text.en: They said they were here to protect us.
Text.zh: 听说他们是来保护我们的。

Text.en: While the Labyrinth traps have claimed countless adventurers, they were not designed with felines in mind.
Text.zh: 虽说迷宫困住了无数冒险者，可他们在设计的时候明显忘了猫儿。

Text.en: "Oh no, I couldn't possibly afford this fine artefact. I'm... just looking."
Text.zh: “唉，我可买不起这么好的艺术品，我只是……看看。”

Text.en: Without the ability to weep, the heart hardens against both the pure and the corrupt.
Text.zh: 心若不会哭泣，就会既反对纯洁，也反对腐坏。

Text.en:
<size:28>{I never truly understood the depths of my insanity,
until I realized the voices beckoning me towards the abyss,
were none other than my own.}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{我从不知道自己有多疯，
直到有一天听到一个声音招呼我走进深渊，
那里只有我一个。}

Text.en: The most pathetic person in the world is someone who has sight, but no vision.
Text.zh: 世界上最可怜的人就是有眼无珠之辈。

Text.en: Through despondent hardships, those strong of spirit shall claim the prize.
Text.zh: 顽强的毅力经受过逆境的磨难，方能获得无上奖赏。

Text.en: <size:28>{They gave the stone life. Over eons spent bathed in Corruption, that life evolved.}
Text.zh: <size:28>{他们赋予顽石生命。在腐坏中浸淫千载之后，生命得到了进化。}

Text.en:
<size:23>{The cities of the Empire were plundered
by those who came after... all but one.}
Text.zh:
<size:23>{帝国的城市都遭到了后来人洗劫……
只有一个例外。}

Text.en: It is said anyone who bears this trinket is the luckiest exile in all of Wraeclast. The gods would disagree, and forever curse any whom give it away.
Text.zh: 大家都说，佩戴这种饰品的流放者是瓦尔克拉斯最幸运的。众神对此很不乐意，于是给胆敢抛弃它的人降下永恒的诅咒。

Text.en: A fond memory fills me with nostalgia; some men would have felt guilt, but I felt no regret over the anguish and fear my ambitions caused.
Text.zh: 美好的回忆勾起了我的乡愁。有些人会感到内疚，可我从不因为惧怕自己的野心而懊悔。

Text.en: <size:30>{Fear a man who is willing to sacrifice anything for power.}
Text.zh: <size:30>{为了力量不择手段的人值得人惧怕。}

Text.en: The largest hooks offer the greatest catch, but often the catch is the hook.
Text.zh: 最大的鱼钩能钓起最大的鱼儿，可他们往往得到的只有鱼钩。

Text.en:
Separate, stories untold.
Together, memories unfold.
Text.zh:
一盘散沙，事不成。
精诚团结，事竟成。

Text.en: <size:28>{No man sees all, but you'll see more than some see in a lifetime.}
Text.zh: <size:28>{没人知道一切，但你这辈子知道的一定比别人多。}

Text.en: The first to travel are often rewarded for their journey, but all the treasure in the world cannot bring back those that are lost.
Text.zh: 头一批敢下海的人往往得到了丰厚的回报，可再多财富也救不活淹死的人。

Text.en: To accept a challenge from the void is merely suicide for the unprepared.
Text.zh: 毫无准备挑战虚空便等于自寻死路。

Text.en: <size:28>{Don't let fool's gold deceive you.}
Text.zh: <size:28>{别让愚人金给骗了。}

Text.en: <size:26>{Hemmingsworth 6:10 - "And the fox gazed out from the brambles, unreachable and smirking."}
Text.zh: <size:26>{海明沃斯第六章第十节- “狐狸从荆棘中探出脑袋，为没人够得着它而发笑。”}

Text.en:
<size:26>{Those Exiles that survive Wraeclast
dream not of home, but of power.}
Text.zh:
<size:26>{在瓦尔克拉斯活下来的流放者
想要的不是家园，而是力量。}

Text.en: He was seeking sanity, but the demons were inside his head.
Text.zh: 他想要理智之道，却败给了心魔。

Text.en: The last thing they saw was the blue light of death.
Text.zh: 他们最后瞥见的，只有死亡之光。

Text.en: "Such fine trinkets show their true value in times of crisis." - Tangmazu
Text.zh: 这些小玩意儿在危机来临时才能彰显价值。——唐玛祖

Text.en:
Cry havoc!
Unleash pandemonium!
Text.zh:
哭吼吧！
施放混沌！

Text.en:
<size:30>{He wandered the bone-filled depths hoping each corner would be his last.
Eventually, he found Light.}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{他在遍地骸骨的深渊里游荡，期望每一个角落都是最后一次探索。
最后，他找到了光。}

Text.en:
<size:30>{"I am not alone in this. I have God by my side! And... you help sometimes, I suppose."
- Sister Cassia}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{我并非孤军奋战。神明与我同在！而且你也肯定会帮我的。"
——卡西娅修女}

Text.en:
"Everything I have done has been for Wraeclast. You would pledge your life to me if you saw things my way... and soon, you will."
- High Templar Venarius
Text.zh:
“我做的一切都是为了瓦尔克拉斯。如果你经历过我的遭遇也会做出同样的选择。而且那很快就会到来。”
——神主维纳留斯

Text.en: Feeding on death, they grew stronger and stronger, until they were ready to hatch...
Text.zh: 死亡的滋养让它们日益强壮，终于等到了孵化的时刻……

Text.en: Good and Evil can be woven together to become something greater than either.
Text.zh: 善恶交织在一起可以化为更伟大的东西。

Text.en:
<size:30>{Savor life's missing pieces.
To complete one's final goal
is to take one's last breath.}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{品味生活中缺失的东西吧。
人生的最终目标
就是咽下最后一口气。}

Text.en: Even the most beautiful of transformations can still have a dark side.
Text.zh: 哪怕最美丽的蜕变也有其黑暗面。

Text.en: Wraeclast will unite. This shall be my great work - the minds of the people made one.
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯必将统一。我将完成如此伟业，将万众凝聚一心。

Text.en: Everything is eroded by time, but sometimes the edges that fade can make a wonderful memory even more beautiful.
Text.zh: 一切都会被时间侵蚀，然而消磨的棱角偶尔会化为无比美妙的回忆。

Text.en: Drink deeply of living rock and be as stone, with blood afire.
Text.zh: 饮下活石，血脉喷张。

Text.en: You mustn't forget: you won't be leaving this place.
Text.zh: 你可不能忘记：你无法离开这里。

Text.en: Why not both?
Text.zh: 不可兼得？

Text.en: A humble piece of wood offers the simplest solution to life's problems: just hit them really, really hard.
Text.zh: 一块不起眼的木头，可以为生活中遇到的问题提供最简单的解决方案:那是狠狠地揍它们。

Text.en: "It was as though my very thoughts were breaking... fracturing into tiny motes to be lost on the wind."
Text.zh: “就好像我的思想全都分崩析离……它们碎裂成微粒，随风而散。”

Text.en: You may be nothing to everybody, but you are everything to somebody.
Text.zh: 你也许在众人眼里不值一提，但在有些人眼里你就是一切。

Text.en: Life has an end, then life begins again.
Text.zh: 生命终有尽头，但那之后又会开始。

Text.en: "When they realize our true intent, they will decry us, assault us, and try to crush what we have built. We must stand tall, Qotra, and make the future our own."
Text.zh: “当他们意识到我们的真正意图时，他们会谴责我们，攻击我们，并试图摧毁我们建立的一切。我们必须傲然屹立，科塔拉，我们要创造自己的未来。”

Text.en: "You seek the power of my strongest potions, but the price will be far higher than you can imagine."
Text.zh: “你想要我最强药剂提供的力量，但代价恐怕远超你的想象。”

Text.en: There are countless people, countless stories, and countless eras, but there is one place that, for a time, each of us loved and was loved. It matters. We matter.
Text.zh: 世上有数不清的众生、故事和时代，但曾经有一个地方，人人都可以爱与被爱。这很重要，我们也重要。

Text.en: "So desperate was I for control, I turned my entire world into a prison. Now you will share in my agony."
Text.zh: “我的控制欲如此强烈，我把我的整个世界都变成了一座监狱。现在你将来分担我的痛苦。”

Text.en: For centuries, they stood vigil in secret over their sleeping goddess, even as contamination oozed out of the decadent Vaal empire.
Text.zh: 数百年来，他们一直秘密守护着沉睡的女神，即使堕落的瓦尔帝国还在遍布污染。

Text.en: When you find what you're looking for, it's never quite right. When you have what you desire, yet you still want more.
Text.zh: 当找到你要找的东西时，它永远不会完全正确。当拥有想要的，你还想要更多。

Text.en: With a single act, years of peace have been shattered. An unknown assassin brings war upon us all.
Text.zh: 仅仅一个举动，多年的和平付之东流。一个不知名的刺客给我们带来了战争。

Text.en: "Virtue gems have facets beyond those that can be seen by the mortal eye. Let us look deeper..."
Text.zh: “美德宝石具有肉眼看不到的层面。让我们更深入观察...”

Text.en: The sun shines not for me. I walk in darkness, never free. Time stopped when you both died in my arms.
Text.zh: 阳光不为我照耀。我在黑暗中行走，从未自由。当你们都死在我怀里时，时间停止了。

Text.en: While you grow, I shall be your knight, and you my squire. One day, you shall be the one who stands and defends our home.
Text.zh: 你成长时，我是你的骑士，你是我的扈从。总有一天，你会成为家园的捍卫者。

Text.en: As death approached, clarity eluded him. The pain he had wrought surrounded him, dragging him into darkness.
Text.zh: 随着死亡的临近，他变得难以捉摸。痛苦包围了他，把他拖进了黑暗。

Text.en:
<size:28>{"Boss said to get rid of him, didn't say not to make a mess."
- Rune The Goon}
Text.zh:
<size:28>{“首领说让他滚蛋，没说不要弄得一团糟。”
— 呆子符文}

Text.en: <size:30>{He who watches the flock is himself watched by those with loyalty beyond measure.}
Text.zh: <size:30>{看着羊群的人自己也被那些无比忠诚的人看着。}

Text.en:
<size:28>{The mask hides his identity
but also makes it hard to read...}
Text.zh: <size:28>{面具隐藏了他的身份，但也让人难以辨认...}

Text.en:
<size:28>{Ancient behemoths swam the
depths of the oceans long
before Man walked these lands.}
Text.zh: <size:28>{早在人类踏上这片土地之前，远古巨兽就在海洋深处游动。}

Text.en: <size:30>{Unbeknown to Atziri's finest thaumaturgists, their loyalty would require far greater devotion than mere research.}
Text.zh: <size:30>{Atziri最优秀的奇术师并不知道，他们的忠诚需要做到比单纯的研究工作更多的奉献。}

Text.en:
For a time, we had something stronger than eldritch evil.
We had each other.
Text.zh: 一段时间内，我们有比邪术更强大的东西。我们拥有彼此。

Text.en:
When you can only have one, which shall it be?
Red?
Blue?
Probably Green.
Text.zh: 当你只能拥有一个的时候，你会选择哪个？红色？蓝色？可能是绿色。

Text.en: When victory is impossible, each of us plays only to delay the end.
Text.zh: 当无法取胜时，我们每个人所做的只是为了拖延结局。

Text.en: My memories tortured me without end. To escape them, I embraced nothingness, but oblivion simply brought a different kind of pain...
Text.zh: 我的记忆无休止地折磨着我。为了逃避，我拥抱虚无，但遗忘只是带来了另一种痛苦...

Text.en: Even in darkness you may find a friend.
Text.zh: 即使在黑暗中，你也能找到朋友。

Text.en: A stereotypical joke may lead to an unexpected bond.
Text.zh: 老掉牙的玩笑中也能蕴藏着最深厚的情谊。

Text.en: Her innocence and grace were enough to tame even the wildest of beasts.
Text.zh: 连最粗野的兽物也要拜倒在她的纯洁与优雅之下。

Text.en:
<size:30>{"Gaze upon the mirrors of what may come and step backward into infinity." 
- Dexion, Thaumetic Aspirant}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{“凝视明镜，可知未来；然后退入无穷境界。” 
- 奇术升华者戴克希翁}

Text.en:
<size:30>{In a land far away lived 
a maiden most fair.
Her smile melted the coldest
of hearts, her song made
men and beast cry, 
her beauty unparalleled;
Divine.}
Text.zh:
<size:30>{遥遥不知何处
有一绝代佳人。
她的微笑如春风化雨，
她的歌声令万物动容，
她的美貌乃举世无双；
圣哉伊人。}

Text.en: The acolytes congratulated the lucky groom for becoming one with the goddess.
Text.zh: 侍僧们恭贺幸运的新郎与女神成为一体。

Text.en: One person's madness is another person's reality.
Text.zh: 一人之疯狂，他人之现实。

Text.en: The Atlas is our prison, and our only defence. She is our only hope, and our certain doom.
Text.zh: 异界乃是我们的牢笼，也是我们最后的堡垒。她乃是我们仅存的希望，也是我们命定的终焉。

Text.en: <size:29>{In the oldest halls of my mind, at the end of a rarely used passage, lies my greatest treasure: one moment of happiness and innocence, pure and untouchable.}
Text.zh: <size:29>{在我记忆的最深处，在那罕有人至的小径之彼侧，有着我最珍贵的宝藏：那是一刹那的幸福与天真；如此纯洁，而又难以企及。}

Text.en: And he shall be heralded by the screams of the damned.
Text.zh: 受巫咒之人的尖啸将昭告他的降临。

Text.en: <size:29>{A little extra flair never hurts.}
Text.zh: <size:29>{奇才天成，多不压身。}

Text.en:
"It's not Karui, Azmeri or even Vaal in origin,
but one thing is certain. It is very, very old."
- Icius Perandus, Antiquities Collection, Item 46
Text.zh:
“它的来源既不是卡鲁、阿兹莫里，也不是瓦尔，
但有一点我能确定。
它真的非常、非常古老。”
- 伊修斯·普兰德斯，《珍奇古玩大典》，藏品第46号

Text.en:
The summer sun dries the soil and burns the leaves.
The autumn rain extinguishes the flames.
The spring bloom shades the wet earth.
Nature is an eternal tug of war.
Text.zh:
夏日烤干地面，燃尽绿叶。
秋雨倾盆而下，扑灭烈焰。
春华四处绽放，遮天蔽日。
自然本身就是一场永恒的战争。

Text.en:
From Weylam to Meredith:
I shall love thee as I love the sea.
Text.zh:
威勒姆至梅瑞迪斯：
我爱这片海洋，但我更爱你。

Text.en: To the wily Kishara's eye, a star brighter than the sun.
Text.zh: 在琪莎拉睿智的眼中，星星比太阳更闪耀。

Text.en:
"The cool blanket of shadows preserves our treasures."
 - Inquisitor Maligaro
Text.zh:
“暗影会轻柔地遮盖住我们的宝藏。”
 - 审判者马雷格罗

Text.en:
"Certain maps can only be rendered in the most vivid of viscera."
 - Inquisitor Maligaro
Text.zh:
“某些地图得用最为精妙的装置才渲染得出来。”
 - 审判者马雷格罗

Text.en:
"A boy becomes a man when he has faced the animal
in his heart and wet the forest floor with its blood."
 - Azmeri Proverb
Text.zh:
“只有直面自己内心的野兽并击败他后，
男孩才能成长为男人。”
 - 阿兹莫里谚语

Text.en:
"The spider is not defined by how
it lives, but by how it kills."
 - Inquisitor Maligaro
Text.zh:
“蜘蛛的价值不在于它繁殖的方式，
而在于它杀戮的手段。”
 - 审判者马雷格罗

Text.en: In all things living, the light burns bright.
Text.zh: 万物内在皆有光明。

Text.en: A passionate fire that consumes the heart.
Text.zh: 热情之火，灼心燃体。

Text.en: The Spirit, it radiates not from above.
Text.zh: 圣灵的光辉从中满溢而出。

Text.en: It is the warmth that draws you ever inward.
Text.zh: 这份暖意引你深入。

Text.en: Where rest the truest colours of life.
Text.zh: 这才是生命真正的颜色。

Text.en: Gold of the organ. Green of the throat. Red of the vein.
Text.zh: 金色的器官、绿色的咽喉、红色的血管。

Text.en: The myriad wet rainbow of the Spirit's domain.
Text.zh: 金色的器官、绿色的咽喉、红色的血管。

Text.en:
"Weakness must be purged lest it
poison the blood of all Karui."
 - Prayer to Tukohama
Text.zh:
“我们必须在软弱荼毒卡鲁血脉前根除它。”
 - 图克哈玛的信徒。

Text.en:
"A stranger's kiss on a moonlit night.
Dost thou believe in love at first sight?"
Text.zh: “月夜的陌生人之吻。你相信一见钟情吗?”

Text.en:
"May the lubbers feel fear in their
nethers, and may our blades follow."
 - Rot-tooth's Rallying Cry
Text.zh:
“对面的傻蛋吓得屁滚尿流，
我们的大刀朝他们的脑袋上砍去。”
 - 烂牙的战歌

Text.en:
"We shall peer at these cosmic wonderments as they
wake and writhe within that deep and nameless dark."
 - Vilenta, 'Miasmeter: A Thesis'
Text.zh:
“宇宙的奥秘即将从漫长的沉眠中觉醒，
而我们将会把它们一一揭晓。”
 - 薇伦塔，《论文：米亚斯表》

Text.en:
Eyes blazing with holy flame, the Pilgrim saw
a great palace descend from the burgeoning sky,
its gates barred against the unfaithful.
Text.zh:
朝圣者看到巨大的宫殿从天而降，
他的眼中闪烁着信仰的光辉，
而宫殿的大门只会为信徒打开。

Text.en:
Wrought from blood and gold, Innocence fashioned a weapon of tempered purity,
capable of banishing darkness, and bringing order to the chaos in men's hearts.
Text.zh:
纯净之神投入鲜血与黄金，
千锤百炼而成一件神兵利器，
它能够驱散黑暗，镇压混乱，带来秩序。

Text.en:
"The sun is a devoted mother. She casts her golden blessings on
the crops of the pious and makes barren the fields of heresy.
Sunlight is belief. Sunlight is life."
 - The Solar Verses
Text.zh:
“阳光是一种信仰，阳光是一种生命。
而太阳就是一位无私的母亲。
无时无刻的将她金色的信仰恩赐在
虔诚的作物和贫瘠的异教土地之上。”
— 《日耀圣经》

Text.en:
"Moonlight is a wise mother. The moon which bathes us in soft,
cooling light, does so with the intent of illuminating our paths.
Moonlight is wisdom. Moonlight is life."
 - The Lunar Verses
Text.zh:
"月光是一种智慧，月光是一种指引。
而月亮就像是一位智慧的母亲，
在黑夜之中，
用轻柔的月光照亮人们前行的道路·"
- 月影圣经

Text.en: "The amulet, it... whispers to me. Horrible things..."
Text.zh: "那个护身符···它正向我耳语着···可怕的故事···"

Text.en:
And mounting her mighty Roc, Garukhan, holy queen of the sky,
descended upon her enemies and their armies of darkness.
Text.zh:
风暴女神，格鲁坎，
驾驭着她那遮天蔽日的大鹏，
向她的敌人和它们的黑暗军团俯冲而下·

Text.en: This gem is only temporary, and only for the tutorial.
Text.zh: 教程专用

Text.en:
Power is a curious thing. 
It can be contained, hidden, locked away, 
and yet it always breaks free.
Text.zh:
力量是一种奇妙的事物。
它可以被保存，被隐藏甚至被封印，
但它总能摆脱束缚。

Text.en:
"And how shall we know a sekhema worthy of rule? 
By the anointing of the Great Roc, under whose wings we grow and soar."
 - Maraketh Coronation Ritual
Text.zh:
"我们该如何分辨丝克玛是否值得我们跟随? 
她会像巨鹏一样庇护我们，让我们生生不息。"
 - 马拉克斯加冕礼

Text.en:
Garukhan sought madness and knowledge amongst the billowing clouds of a 
blackened sky. A vulture of pride, she would not be refused, and so the
stratosphere divulged unto her eldritch secrets of its tumultuous past.
Text.zh:
格鲁坎在漆黑的天空之中寻找着她自己的疯狂。
为了荣耀和骄傲，她不向一切低头。
她不止要成为风暴女神，
她真正想要的是掌握整片天空·

Text.en: "Blow it all down! The emperor must have his gems, no matter the cost!"
Text.zh:
“都炸掉！ 无论付出怎样的代价，
我们都必须为大帝弄到这些宝石。

Text.en:
Nobility once thought to capture the Basilisks and keep them as exotic pets.
This only served to fatten the vileness of their hearts, and with a hateful
acid that festered inside, the creatures became soured to their very bones.
Text.zh:
曾经有贵族想着要征服蛇怪并将它们驯养成为剧毒宠物。
最终那个贵族被毒液所融化，
只留下一片黑色的遗骸。
最恶毒的心灵最后成就了最恶毒的身躯。

Text.en:
The King looked upon images swirling atop the ancient stone dial.
"You look peaceful" he told his bride-to-be, "So peaceful, asleep in our wedding bed."
But in phantasm, she did not slumber, and what he saw was not a place for dreams,
rather, a wooden box... about to be closed and lowered into earthy depths.
 - Maraketh Legend
Text.zh:
国王看着古老石盘上的图画不停盘旋。
“你真安详啊，”他对未来的新娘说道。“安详到在我们的婚床上沉眠。”
然而在幻象之中，她却并非睡眠，而他看到的也非心中所见。
他只看到一具木棺……即将被深邃的泥土掩埋。
- 马拉克斯的传说

Text.en:
"One should not entice the storm; one cannot invite the storm, or give warrant
to its tyrant-soul. We survive only by appeasing the storm, with gifts and
offerings of adoration. We hope that, in its mercy, it shall pass us by."
- Tempestology of the Maraketh, Repudion IV
Text.zh:
“人不该招惹风暴；无法邀约风暴，更不能为其暴虐之灵提供借口。我们唯有以贡品与崇敬之祭奠安抚风暴，方能存续。我们心怀希冀，盼其仁慈，愿它从我们身旁绕行。”
——《马拉克斯风暴学》，驳论卷四

Text.en:
Famous for her many lovers, those that did not
come willing to Atziri, came by liquid persuasion.
Text.zh: 对于所有无视阿兹里女王美貌的人，都最终喝下了它。

Text.en:
"T'gnagn iia y r'ngusla ph'tu Tsoatha nga mekghul'an."
 - Indecipherable verse
Text.zh:
T'gnagn iia y r'ngusla ph'tu Tsoatha nga mekghul'an.
— （无法解读的诗句）

Text.en:
"Worry not for the heathens. They are devoid of soul.
Made vessels of wrath, they are damned by holy design."
- High Templar Avarius
Text.zh:
"那些没有纯净灵魂的异教徒。
他们和死人或者是行尸走肉并无区别。" 
- 神主 阿瓦留斯

Text.en:
In the end, time comes for all of us.
For some, it comes much sooner.
Text.zh:
到最后，时间的力量会作用在所有人身上。
只是对于有的人来说，它作用的更快而已·

Text.en:
"Would you leave an orchard of apples to rot unharvested?
A cemetery is no different." 
-Kadavrus, Surgeon to the Umbra
Text.zh:
"你会放弃一片果园选择一片荒芜的未垦之地吗? 
对于坟墓来说他们并无区别吧·" 
—— 黑影薛朗的助手 凯达弗斯

Text.en: Like lightning, the Ezomyte cavalry need never strike twice.
Text.zh:
一道闪电能摧毁一切，
艾兹麦铁骑的一阵冲杀就能结束战斗·

Text.en:
The monstrous men of yore used bows too,
theirs were just bigger.
Text.zh:
古老而庞大的身躯，
曾扣开致命而悦耳的弓弦。

Text.en:
Countless men proclaimed themselves the masters of death,
and each one succumbed to it.
Text.zh:
无数人曾宣告自己是死亡的主人，
而他们每个人却又最终屈服于死亡。

Text.en:
"Creation is the act of making the intangible tangible.
To work backwards, to make the tangible intangible,
is to step into the mind of a god."
- Doryani, Queen's Thaumaturgist
Text.zh:
“创造，是将无形化为有形的艺术。  
反其道而行，从有形提炼出无形的规则，  
则踏入了神的领域。”   
- 女王奇术师多里亚尼

Text.en:
Though his mind decayed with each passing wave,
the Brine King stood steadfast in his hatred,
and wore it like a shell, protecting himself 
from his failure to sire a worthy heir.
Text.zh:
惊海之王的思绪随波涛不断起伏，
他将自己的身体和思想都藏身在外壳之中，
保护着自己一族的血脉和意识通过自己的身躯传承下来。

Text.en:
The fish-wives of Tsoatha rejoiced loudly as
the Brine King descended into the great abyss,
making merry while his blasphemous offspring
slithered 'neath the icy waters...
Text.zh:
索亚格斯的妻妾们大声欢呼，
惊海之王终于坠入了深渊，
他已葬身冰冷的海水中，
再也无法繁衍他亵渎神灵的后代们……

Text.en:
Slumbering in the breach between worlds,
the Brine King dreamt of a day he would rise,
in triumph from the shivering depths and take
back the Kingdom he once so ruthlessly culled.
Text.zh:
在世界的边缘沉睡，
惊海之王一直等待着新时代的到来，
终于在这一天，他从震动的深渊中惊醒，
从他的子民手中重新取回了他的王国·

Text.en:
There is a story held by the ancient Vaal, in which the
beautiful seductress Arakaali strode through towns, stealing
the hearts of men, and turning to ash their lovers' desires.
- "Legends of the Vaal" by Eramir, Scholar at Theopolis
Text.zh:
"古老的瓦尔文明曾传下这样一个故事，
充满美丽与诱惑的阿拉卡力穿过了城镇，
偷走了所有男人的心，
粉碎了所有女人的情。"
- 摘选自"瓦尔传说" 
艾米尔，来自费奥普罗斯的学者

Text.en:
When the cold was at its strongest, Arakaali came,
and like a farmer leading cattle to the slaughter,
she drove her victims into the outer darkness.
- "Legends of the Vaal" by Eramir, Scholar at Theopolis
Text.zh:
在最寒冷的时候，阿拉卡力来了，
就像一个农民带着牛去屠宰场一样，
她和她的仰慕者们走进了寂静的黑暗之中。
- 摘选自"瓦尔传说" 
艾米尔，来自费奥普罗斯的学者

Text.en:
Records of Arakaali's violent reign suggest she kept her citizens
sedated with 'juices of lust' and venoms of the spider. Perhaps her
guise of seductress was in fact her way of farming those 'juices'.
- "Legends of the Vaal" by Eramir, Scholar at Theopolis
Text.zh:
有关亚拉卡力的史料记载其在统治王国期间曾使用“欲望汁液”和蜘蛛毒液控制国民。或许她那妖艳的伪装实质是收获这些“汁液”的手段。
- 《瓦尔的传说》，西奥波利斯学者艾米尔著

Text.en:
The mud is cracked and dry. The fields now burn beneath the blistering sun.
Even our children are dying in the streets! We've angered Solaris and so
we pay tribute to appease her fiery heart...
Text.zh:
泥土干枯开裂，绿色的田野化为炙热的地狱。
就连孩童都无法逃脱横尸街头的厄运！我们惹怒了日曜，为此我们需要献出祭品，抚平她的怒火……

Text.en:
When Solaris is angered, there must always be sacrifice. Our women
have taken to mourning, they drag themselves through ash and glass,
not wanting to give up our youth to the all-consuming flame.
Text.zh: 只有祭品才能平息日曜的愤怒。女人们悲鸣着穿过灰烬和碎瓦，不愿成为活祭，葬身于烈焰之中。

Text.en:
To take from the sun is to invite her wrath, and we've more than warranted her curse.
The granaries were vacant, so we lessened our tribute to the sun thinking it would
save our children, but they will die now regardless.
Text.zh:
享受太阳带来的恩惠的同时，也必须承受太阳的怒火，我们深知这一点。
粮仓已空，因此我们不得不减少献出的祭品，希望
太阳能够网开一面，然而她还是会夺走孩子们的性命。

Text.en:
Once beneath a moonlit sky, Lunaris bathed her child in the ocean's tide.
Though the waters were frigid with icy death, the boy was sick and
couldn't be healthy until he was silent, clean and still...
Text.zh:
月影曾在月光的照耀下用海潮洗涤她孩子的身体。
然而，海水冰冷刺骨，那个男孩的体质更是虚弱不堪，他的呻吟一直到他变成一具冰冷的尸体后才停止下来……

Text.en:
And so it was, that Lunaris, in her compulsion to nurture and protect,
killed her son beneath the light of a waning moon. She stood silent, in
trial before her tribe, as her fiery sister fought in vain to defend her...
Text.zh: 于是，月影那温柔的关怀反而成为了杀死她儿子的凶器。月光下的她静静矗立着，在部落里接受审判，而她那性如烈火的姐姐拼命地为她辩护，却不知自己的努力都是徒劳……

Text.en:
But Lunaris refused to admit any wrongdoing. She'd saved her child, had she not?
He was safe now, buried beneath the dirt. Conviction gnarled around her fragile mind,
and in her pride she rose to seat a deathless throne.
Text.zh:
但月影拒绝承认自己的过错。她认为自己赐予了儿子救赎。
他现在深埋在泥土之下，不会再遭遇任何危险。这样的想法
扭曲了月影，
使她成为了一个傲慢的暴君。

Text.en:
When the demon Goat plays his flute,
And cloven hooves clip-clop your path,
Pray the gods will hold you mute,
And bless the fire on your hearth.
Text.zh:
羊魔奏笛，
横刀挡路，
朝天默祈，
灶火永固。

Text.en:
The earth shakes, moon eclipses black.
Graveyards quake, crops turn to ash,
as the grieving mother weeps.
Oceans boil and wind ravages home. 
We feel famine inside our bones,
as the grieving mother, Gruthkul weeps.
Text.zh:
大地震颤，月黑之时。
墓碑晃动，谷粒烬蚀，
犹母丧子，戚泪如斯。
大海沸腾，狂风飞逝。
饥馑透骨，此苦不已，
犹母丧子，其戚惨极。

Text.en:
In nature, there are many feelings exclusive only to man. But terror is not one.
Terror flourishes in our world, transcending all things. It is the bedrock on
which our great cosmos is formed. I am Yugul and I deign to understand this
mystery, to explore its depths and unlock its secrets...
Text.zh:
人类拥有的许多情感都是其他动物所没有的。然而，恐惧却是所有生灵所共享的。
恐惧就像自然现象，自创始之初便一直存在着。我是约格尔，我发誓必将解开恐惧的奥秘，探索其深意，揭开其谜团……

Text.en:
"So it's true." Shakari thought, as she took her final breath.
"My father, Sin, wishes me dead. And he has sent his rabid dog to do it..."
Text.zh: “原来真的是这样，”纱卡利心想，随后便咽下了最后一口气，“我的父亲，罪恶，真的想杀我，还派走狗来替他干脏活……”

Text.en:
Tukohama stood in the door, blocking the sunlight
from Hikatomanga and his family. The Father of War's
retribution was inevitable, for it is known - man
cannot cheat the gods without consequence...
Text.zh: 图克哈马站在门边，夺去了席卡托曼加一家最后的希望。战争之父的报复是无法避免的，原因很简单——人类只能欺瞒神祇一时，无法骗过它们一世……

Text.en:
"Lock the gates and build the walls!" came the rallying cry,
"Let those of Ralakesh take care of those of Ralakesh!"
- The God Behind The Wall
Text.zh:
“锁上大门，筑起城墙！” 战吼在飘扬。 
“让万面之主拉克斯的奴仆们自相残杀” 
 - 城墙后的守护神

Text.en:
Garukhan wished once more for the skies. If she were to die,
she wanted it to be up there in the clouds. This world had never
been her home. She required greater things. She required adventure.
But die she would, here in the dirt...
Text.zh: 格鲁坎向往着天空。即便是死，也想死在云端。她从没有将这个世界当成过她的家园。她不需要这样寒碜的家。她需要的是波澜壮阔的冒险。但她终究会归于尘土。

Text.en:
Ryslatha was flushed with pride as she watched her mindless spawn
mature. Sharpening their mandibles, her children slithered from the hive,
eager to devour all the creatures that frequented the ancient woods.
Text.zh: 看着她那无脑的后裔逐渐成熟，瑞斯拉萨的心中充满了自豪。她的子嗣打磨着尖牙，从巢穴中钻出，想要吞噬古代森林中的一切。

Text.en:
In the shadow of war,
all are eclipsed.
Text.zh:
在战争的阴影之下，
一切都将黯然失色。

Text.en: We are ever the makers of our own undoing.
Text.zh: 一切的错误与失败都源自于我们自己。

Text.en: Man creates where nature cannot.
Text.zh: 自然无能为力，人类却能创造。

Text.en:
"Whatever these maps are, it is clear they are dangerous. Send in only
test subjects, no guards or researchers." 
- Administrator Qotra
Text.zh:
“不管这些地图是什么，它们都绝对很危险。
只能派实验对象去，不能派守卫和研究员。”
——总管科塔拉

Text.en:
For the immortal, what is the difference
between an age and an instant?
Text.zh:
对于永恒的神灵而言，
一个世纪与一瞬之间又有什么差别呢。

Text.en: Be careful where you put your finger.
Text.zh: 当心你的手指，别放在不该放的地方。

Text.en: In every piece of prose, lies a tiny spark of magic.
Text.zh:
在每一篇诗文之中，都隐藏着一丝魔力。  
它们终将汇聚一起，迎来崭新的时代。

Text.en:
In the years that followed the twisted sky,
cold winds cloaked Wraeclast in snow.
The pure-white blanket masked a black heart
that lay dormant deep beneath the earth.
Text.zh:
苍天破碎经年累月，
瓦尔大地冰霜席卷。
纯白之毯覆盖黑土，
黑土之下恶魔蠢动。

Text.en:
The skilled know how to learn.
The talented know who to learn from.
Text.zh:
经验丰富的人知道学习的方式。
天资聪颖的人知道学习的对象。

Text.en:
"Twelve test subjects were expended in the production of Prototype #800.
Was it worth the expense? Only time will tell."
Text.zh:
“八百号实验底子的生产过程中消耗了十二个实验对象。
这样的代价值得吗？只有时间能证明。”

Text.en:
"Every day we choose safety or knowledge. 
Live and stagnate, or risk death for truth.
I would rather see the end of the Vaal
than help it tread water." 
- Doryani, Queen's Thaumaturgist
Text.zh:
“每一天我们都在安逸和知识中抉择，
平静安逸的或者，或是冒死寻求真知。
我宁愿看到瓦尔帝国的终结，也不希望它如履薄冰般苟延残喘。”
- 女王奇术师多里亚尼

Text.en:
"What cruelty it is, that to be judged a success,
such great work must go uncredited." 
- Coralito, Brotherhood of Silence
Text.zh:
“这个世界最残酷的是，直到成功之前，  
再伟大的使命也必须面对诋毁与质疑。”  
- 克拉里多， 沉默兄弟会

Text.en:
Your memories are a bridge to the past.
Let no one cross.
Text.zh:
你的记忆是通向过去的桥梁。
但却无人能够通过。

Text.en: One's burden is another's gift.
Text.zh: 一人眼中担，他人心中宝。

Text.en: Dance like dust on the desert wind.
Text.zh: 舞动，如同沙暴中的尘埃。

Text.en:
The Maraketh fear very little, 
but they dare not whisper the name
of the demon that flies on Winter's gales.
Text.zh:
马拉克斯人无所畏惧，  
但却不敢低声说出那  
在凛冬狂风之中飞舞的恶魔。

Text.en:
Through droughts, fires, floods and frost,
the ancient giants stand resolute,
while deep in the rich, dark earth,
their grasp stretches ever farther.
Text.zh:
干旱，火灾，洪水和霜冻，  
让远古的巨人站稳脚跟，  
而在深厚肥沃的黑土中，  
他们的根茎已向更远处延伸。

Text.en: Let the unrepentant be dragged ever downwards by the weight of their sins.
Text.zh: 让冥顽不灵者深陷罪恶的泥沼吧。

Text.en: Enough mice can kill a wolf.
Text.zh: 足够的肉沫也能撑死一匹饿狼。

Text.en: Hurt as you would be hurt.
Text.zh: 伤害他人的同时也意味着伤害自己。

Text.en: Even the dead serve the Lightless.
Text.zh: 即使亡魂也为黑暗所统治。

Text.en:
"Ever watched a man crush another man's skull?
Hard to feel hope after that."
Text.zh:
“你可见过一个人彻底的碾碎另一个人的信仰？
那是令人彻底失去希望的过程。”

Text.en:
Hold in your hand the darkness
and never will the light blind you.
Text.zh:
手中紧攥黑暗，
光明便无所适从。

Text.en:
The eyes of the living hold a glimmer of hope.
Don't waste it.
Text.zh:
活人的眼中有那么一丝希望。
不要浪费这份希望。

Text.en: Look to the darkness for nourishment and you will never go hungry.
Text.zh: 于黑暗中迷失的人是不会感到饥饿的。

Text.en:
"Eyes of black, three by three, staring ever darker into me..."
- Researcher Arn
Text.zh:
“黑眼睛，三对三，乌黑发亮朝我看……”
——研究员阿恩

Text.en:
Land and sea did not dissuade,
nor did the Kelpie's maw;
the wild beast crossed half the world
to sate his hungry claw.
Text.zh:
大地和海洋都不会劝诫寻死者，
魔兽更不会；
世间的野兽只会
将他们大快朵颐。

Text.en:
Candles with the brightest light
Bring life to death and day to night.
But lovely as the glow may seem,
Their light is fleeting, as a dream...
Text.zh:
郎朗烛光
照亮黑夜。
光芒虽亮，
转瞬即逝……

Text.en:
As she was sentenced to death, Doedre was silent.
As she was tied to the stake, Doedre was silent.
As the flames licked away her life, Doedre was silent.
But Wraeclast had not heard the last of her.
Text.zh:
被判了死刑的德瑞沉默不语。
被绑到木桩上的德瑞一言不发。
被烈火吞噬的德瑞依旧没有吭声。
因为从今以后她的声音会传遍瓦尔克拉斯。

Text.en:
"The Council have a great many secrets.
Some are better kept that way, Cospri."
- Mauritius, the Iron Heart
Text.zh:
“议会拥有许多秘密。
其中一些最好永远不见天日，卡斯普里。”
- 钢铁之心莫里提斯

Text.en:
Though its body was locked in stone,
its essence wandered the infinite,
learning, and preparing.
Text.zh:
身陷囹圄，
精华不灭，
永世长存。

Text.en:
There is no darker void than the hollow
ache of love disremembered...
Text.zh: 世界上最黑暗的虚空，就是忘记爱的痛苦……

Text.en:
With the patience of a prowling lion,
the Shade watched the Scholar.
Text.zh:
暗影耐心地监视着学者，
不带丝毫急切。

Text.en:
One by one, they stood their ground against a creature 
they had no hope of understanding, let alone defeating,
and one by one, they became a part of it.
Text.zh:
他们一个又一个地挺身而出，
与他们无从理解更无从对抗的怪物战斗，
然后前赴后继地成为怪物的一份子。

Text.en:
Reaching nature's limits, 
many sought power in the unnatural.
Text.zh:
在达到自然的极限后，
许多人都尝试突破自然的桎梏。

Text.en:
They hoped that, trapped in its prison,
the creature would age and perish.
But time would not touch the fiend.
Text.zh:
人们希望，那只被困在牢狱中的怪物
会衰老死去。
但时间没有对这只恶魔造成丝毫影响。

Text.en:
Their last thoughts were not of their friends,
or their families, or their homes.
Their last thoughts were untethered from reality,
and bathed in fear and ferocity.
Text.zh:
他们最后想到的不是亲朋好友，
也不是祖国家园。
他们在最后一刻，
感受到的只有纯粹的恐惧和绝望。

Text.en:
All at once, a calmness would encompass them,
and all at once, it would be torn away,
unveiling forms no sound mind could grasp.
Text.zh:
他们曾冷静过一瞬，
然而这份冷静很快便被夺走，
空留破碎的心灵。

Text.en:
Men always fell for her honeyed eyes,
and broke when they hit the ground.
Text.zh:
男人往往会被她的媚眼诱惑，
成为她那无尽爱欲的牺牲品。

Text.en:
Lunaris crossed silently, shining and full, 
Softly night left her, alone and forlorn.
Solaris rose to meet her, feeling her pull.
'Neath the midday sun, the night was reborn.
Text.zh:
月影沐浴着月光，沉静地穿行着，
孤身一人离开了温柔的黑夜。
日曜起身与她会面，感受到了她的压力。
黑夜将在艳阳之下重生。

Text.en:
When the world spins out of control,
the safest place to be is in the centre.
Text.zh:
当世界开始旋转，
最安全的地方就在它的中央。

他们挺过了无数岁月，
身上满是岁月留下的痕迹；
而这也是他们凭借肉体书写的记忆。

Text.en:
By wind and wing they are carried,
Their fates not theirs to control,
Yet the fates of others dragged in their wake.
Text.zh:
他们乘着清风，展翅高飞。
虽然他们无法主宰自己的命运，
但却可以牵动他人的命运。

Text.en:
Lava bled from the earth and,
after a time, cooled and hardened.
The world protecting its beating heart 
with a thick, black scab.
Text.zh:
破土而出的岩浆，
经过一段时间便会冷却变硬。
岩浆就是这个世界的血液，
它只能借此结痂保护自己的心脏。

Text.en: Darkness never stops feeding.
Text.zh: 黑暗永不寂灭。

Text.en:
"Your name is Demon Slayer? Really? Good name for rotgut, that."
She said: I prefer "The Goddess Of Swords". Much more dignified. And accurate.
"Demon Slayer The Cheap And Nasty it is then. Burns going down, burns coming up?"
She said: Every single time, Charan. And still...you'll bathe me, and you'll drink my love. Until you can't.
Text.zh:
“你的名字叫鬼弑？真的吗？听上去就像是什么酒鬼的佩剑呢”
她说道：我更喜欢“女神之剑”这个名字。更加庄严与准确，
“鬼弑，多低劣和粗俗的名字啊。你想与我的烈焰一起燃烧，熄灭？”
她说道：“过去的每一次，查兰，直到现在...
以及你将与我一起面对的未来，享受我对你的爱吧，直到你不再。”

Text.en:
"...A golden machine was found broken in the ruins of Wraeclast. 
Believing it to contain some dark infernal secret, 
High Templar Venarius asked that I restore it 
and weaponize whatever powers it might contain..."
Text.zh:
“...一台金色的仪器被从瓦尔克拉斯的遗迹里找到。
人们相信里面包含了一些黑暗力量的秘密，
时任圣堂教宗维纳瑞斯要求我修复它
并将其中包含的任意力量作为军事用途...”

Text.en:
"...Though the device seemed to be mostly functioning, 
something important appeared to be missing...
...I worked tirelessly in search of answers, until finally, 
at the base of that cruel device, I fell into a deep, deep sleep..."
Text.zh:
“……尽管该设备大部分功能完好，
但似乎欠缺某样重要的零件……
……我日以继夜地搜寻着答案，终于支撑不住
倒在这台邪恶的设备旁边，昏睡过去……”

Text.en:
"...I awoke in the most beautiful of places, and as I wandered this strange new land, 
I felt a growing sense of realization that I was not alone. 
A Shade, a whisper of embodied smoke, spoke to me through thoughts and pictures.
I found myself enthusiastically volunteering information in detail of Oriath..."
Text.zh:
“……等我睁开双眼，却看到了绝美的奇景。于是我便在
这奇妙的新天地散起步来，
这时我渐渐意识到，这里不止我一人。
一抹如黑烟般的暗影透过思维和图画向我低语。
我发现自己竟不自觉地向它吐露奥瑞亚的详细信息……”

Text.en:
"...The Shade knew of the device - a doorway between my world
and the dreamlands. It offered to help me rebuild the missing part, 
to create a conduit between this beautiful land and Oriath. 
All that the Shade asked of me was to return the favour..."
Text.zh:
“……暗影知道那台设备是用来干嘛的——它是连接我的世界和梦中世界的桥梁。它答应帮我重做那个遗失的零件，帮我将这片乐园和奥瑞亚连接起来。
作为回报，暗影只要求我帮它一个小忙……”

Text.en:
"...Every night, I found myself asleep at the foot of the strange device, 
awoken to the reality of another world within my own.
The Shade taught me how to shape and build things from my imagination,
how to transport such phantasmagorical treasures back into the world of man..."
Text.zh:
“……每天晚上，我都会在那个古怪的设备旁睡着，醒来时就发现自己已身在梦中世界。
暗影教会了我运用想象力塑造事物的技巧，
以及将这些奇幻的珍宝转移到现实世界的能力……”

Text.en:
"...The missing part of the great device had finally been formed.
The Shade requested its one favour. I was shown images of the past,
the Shade as King of the dreamlands, the hateful Watchers of Decay...
It led me deep into a cave, to a statue pierced by a sword..."
Text.zh:
“……我们终于制成了这台神奇的设备缺失的零件。
暗影告诉我，报答它的时刻到了。她向我展示了过去的景象，向我展示了她身为这个世界的王者时的记忆，以及腐朽监视者有多么可憎……

Text.en:
"Pull the blade from my chest. Free me!" the Shade imposed.
But doubt entered my mind, and the Shade flew into a fury!
I felt my mind torn asunder as images of murder and mutilation were forced upon me. 
I fled in a panic, cursing myself for ever trusting such a strange creature so blindly..."
Text.zh:
“速速拔出凶刃，放我自由！”暗影对我循循善诱。
但我却迟疑了，然后暗影突然发怒了！
我的脑海中突然出现了无数暴力的景象。
仓皇逃窜的我不禁后悔起来，果然不应该盲目相信这么古怪的存在……”

Text.en:
"...Months have now passed since the Shade revealed its true nature.
Each morning, I lock myself inside my study, searching for some knowledge 
that might save us. I have found at last, some information on the Watchers of Decay. 
The truth is so unutterable that I hesitate, even now, to write it down..."
Text.zh:
“……在暗影显露真面目后过了数月。
每天早晨，我都会把自己关在书房内，搜寻着能用来自救的知识。最后，我终于发现了腐朽监视者留下的信息。
这真相实在令人瞠目结舌，以至于时至今日我还会犹豫要不要将它记载下来……”

Text.en:
"...They called it the Elder. A creature of malignant madness... it cannot be killed.
The Watchers built the device to lock tight its kingdom, bringing with them the
blade - 'Starforge'. A weapon to give the Elder eternal rest. But the Shade...
I must find a way to put a stop to it before it finds a path to freedom..."
Text.zh:
“……他们将它称之为裂界者。它是一只由恶意和狂念构成的怪物……而且无法被杀死。
监视者们制造这一设备的初衷便是为了将它关在梦境国度中，以便找到神器——“塑星者之剑”，使其陷入永眠。但暗影……
我必须在暗影重获自由前想办法阻止它……”

Text.en:
"...I've spent every waking hour since, trying to find a way to end this unholy Elder.
Within my secret study, I've begun work on a device of my own, tinkering and shaping.
When I'm finished, this Elder will never bother our world again.
The creature cannot be killed, but maybe... it can be exiled..."
Text.zh:
“……自那之后我便不眠不休地寻找能够制裁这个邪恶的裂界者
的办法。
我在秘密书房内开始自己设计并组装一台设备。
只要能完成这台设备，裂界者就无法再次威胁到现实世界。
虽然我无法杀死那只怪物，但我或许……可以将它流放……”

Text.en:
"...Lack of progress on the device has given the High Templar cause for suspicion.
Clapped in irons, I was lead off to the Theopolis prisons for my insubordination.
I must find my way from these chains at all costs. The Elder is coming for us.
Only I can save us from this blasphemy that will soon befall Oriath..."
Text.zh:
“……研究的停滞不前引起了圣堂教宗的怀疑。
我被判渎职罪，关押在西奥波利斯监狱中。
我必须不惜一切代价逃狱，裂界者对这个世界虎视眈眈。
只有我能够阻止这个看上了奥瑞亚的恶魔……”

Text.en:
"...Venarius, that bastard... he marched me through the streets.
His men beat me within an inch of death... I... I told him everything. 
He demanded that I take all of us into the dreamlands, to meet with the Elder.
Using the device, we stepped through, setting foot in the Atlas of Worlds..."
Text.zh:
“……维纳瑞斯这个愚蠢之徒……把我拉出监狱游街示众，让手下
把我打得奄奄一息……逼我……告诉了他一切。
他命我带上他和手下前往梦境国度，与裂界者会面。
于是我便开启了设备，带着他们穿越到了异界图鉴中……”

Text.en:
"...It wasn't long before we were met by the Shade itself.
The very essence of the Elder, demanding of me in visions to
tell it why I had returned. But before I could answer,
Venarius stepped forward and greeted the phantom..."
Text.zh:
“……不大一会儿我们便见到了暗影。
这个裂界者的精魂在我的脑海中质问我为何回来。但我还来不及回答，
维纳瑞斯就走上前去向他致意……”

Text.en:
"...Before we knew it, we stood at the base of that blasphemous effigy.
"Pull the sword from my chest." The Elder imposed, and Venarius didn't hesitate.
The earth shook in the face of the Elder's reunion with its body. The true scope of the
Elder befell the High Templar and his men, and as it began to feed, I began to run..."
Text.zh:
“……仅仅一瞬之后，我们便站在了这个邪恶的神祇面前。
‘速速拔出此凶刃。’裂界者诱惑道，维纳瑞斯立刻照做了。
于是，裂界者的意识重新回到了他的体内，大地开始颤动，重回原形的裂界者碾碎了圣堂教宗和他的手下，开始大快朵颐，而我只能匆忙逃窜……”

Text.en:
"...I fear this to be the end. Not just the end for me, but the end of all things. 
The Elder has been freed. Soon it will feast on me... on the rest of the world.
It was too late for me. The Elder had to be stopped. I knew of the shaping powers.
Only I could have a hope at defeating the Elder..."
Text.zh:
“……我害怕这可能就是一切的终焉。我会命丧于此，整个世界也会沦陷于裂界者的魔掌。
我已经逃不掉了，但必须有人能阻止裂界者，而我正好拥有塑造
物体的能力。
只有我还尚存击败裂界者的希望……”

Text.en:
I spent my life shining a light on truth...
but some things should remain in darkness,
locked away for all eternity.
Text.zh:
我用自己的生命之光揭开真理…
但有些东西应该永远留在黑暗中，
与世隔绝。

Text.en: Be not stirred by the Void.
Text.zh: 不要被虚无所扰乱。

Text.en: Let madness take control.
Text.zh: 让最原始的疯狂作出抉择。

Text.en:
Where the body's limits begin,
the mind's limits end.
Text.zh:
肉体决定了你的起点，
而灵魂决定了你的终点。

Text.en:
Blurred is the boundary
between creator and destroyer.
Text.zh:
创造者和毁灭者之间，
只有一步之遥。

Text.en:
A weapon born of nothingness,
can only create more nothingness.
Text.zh:
由虚无而生的武器，
只能创造更多的虚无。

Text.en: Even emptiness may be harnessed.
Text.zh:
即使空无一物的虚空，
也能被掌控。

Text.en:
The First of the Deep was the First of All.
It was He who conquered the waves,
who stood guard as land rose from sea.
Text.zh:
深海初子是一切的源头，是一切的开始。
是他在时间的初始征服了海洋，
是他在陆地升起时在海中仰望。

Text.en:
When the rains come, we cower beneath shelter.
The First of the Deep teaches us
that we should seek no shelter but ourselves.
Text.zh:
下雨的时候，当我们躲避雨滴的时候。
深海初子教导我们，
除了我们自己的身躯，
我们不应该寻求其他的庇护。

Text.en:
The stone that stays in the waves soon crumbles to sand.
The First of the Deep teaches us to watch the tides
and choose our moments to move wisely.
Text.zh:
留在海浪里的石头很快就会碎成沙子。
深海初子教会我们观察潮汐的律动，
选择时机明智地行动。

Text.en:
When the land rose above the sea, The First of the Deep did not move.
When the sky turned to fire, The First of the Deep did not perish.
We must remember our place, and play to our strengths.
Text.zh:
当陆地升出海面之时，深海初子没有移动。
当天空变成了火海之时，深海初子没有消亡。
我们必须记住自己的位置，发挥我们的优势。

Text.en:
A general may carry his men to greatness, 
or be dragged beneath the mire by their burden.
Text.zh:
一个将军可以带领士兵走向伟大，
也能被士兵拖进死亡的泥潭。

Text.en:
The First of the Plains was the First of the Hunt
It was she who showed us that there is honour
in waiting in the shadows and picking your moment.
Text.zh:
大地初子是狩猎的源头，是战斗的开始。
是她向我们展示了什么是荣誉，
是在阴影中耐心等待，等待你的时刻到来。

Text.en:
"Attempt #120: the prototype has finally achieved power similar to the original
without breaking all the bones of the test subject. A rousing success."
Text.zh:
“第一百二十次实验：该实验底子终于表现出了原版的能力。
它没有把实验对象的骨头全打断。很成功。”

Text.en:
It is a fool who strikes the turtle's shell.
The First of the Plains teaches us to exploit weaknesses,
and where no weakness can be found, to create one.
Text.zh:
只有傻瓜才会去攻击中了乌龟的硬壳。
大地初子教会我们如何利用弱点，
而如果没有弱点，就自己创造一个。

Text.en:
A hungry beast wastes no energy. 
Every strike, no matter how small, must work towards victory.
The First of the Plains teaches us 
that the largest prey can still be whittled away.
Text.zh:
饥饿的野兽不会浪费能量。
每一次行动，无论多么细微，都必须是为了胜利而前进。
大地初子教会我们。
再庞大的猎物也能被逐渐蚕食。

Text.en:
A secret is a weapon your enemies do not have.
When the First of the Plains hunted, she moved in silence and acted quickly.
To do otherwise is to arm your foes.
